description:
# Pointer Event Dispatch Pipeline

Implement pointer event dispatch per input-model/spec.md.

## Spec Requirements (with line numbers from spec.md)
- **Requirement: Pointer Event Types** (lines 278-280, spec.md) — PointerDownEvent, PointerUpEvent, PointerMoveEvent, PointerEnterEvent, PointerLeaveEvent, ClickEvent, DoubleClickEvent, ContextMenuEvent, PointerCancelEvent. All carry tile_id, node_id, device_id, coordinates (node-local and display-space), modifiers, timestamp_mono_us.
- **Requirement: Event Dispatch Flow** (lines 304-306, spec.md) — Five-phase pipeline: Stage 1 Input Drain (< 500µs p99, attach timestamps, enqueue raw input), Stage 2 Local Feedback (< 500µs p99, hit-test against bounds snapshot, update HitRegionLocalState, produce SceneLocalPatch), compositor thread Stages 3-4 (apply SceneLocalPatch, forward to event router), event router resolves owning agents (< 2ms from Stage 2 completion), network thread delivers EventBatch on agent gRPC session stream. Stages 1+2 on main thread with no locks on mutable scene graph.
- **Requirement: Event Routing Resolution** (lines 315-317, spec.md) — NodeHit maps to tile's lease owner session; TileHit maps to tile's lease owner session; Chrome events handled locally; Passthrough events pass to desktop in overlay mode or discarded in fullscreen.
- **Requirement: Hit-Test Performance** (lines 263-265, spec.md) — < 100µs p99 for 50 tiles. Hit-test order: chrome layer first (always wins), content layer tiles by z-order descending, within tile nodes in reverse tree order (last child first), first HitRegionNode whose bounds contain point wins.
- **Requirement: HitRegionNode Primitive** (lines 248-249, spec.md) — sole interactive primitive in v1: bounds, interaction_id, accepts_focus, accepts_pointer, auto_capture, release_on_up, cursor_style, tooltip (500ms hover), event_mask, accessibility metadata, local_style.
- **Requirement: Event Dispatch to Agent Latency** (lines 356-358, spec.md) — hit-test + session lookup + serialization + enqueue < 2ms from Stage 2 completion.
- **Requirement: Protobuf Schema for Input Events** (lines 367-369, spec.md) — InputEnvelope (22-variant oneof), EventBatch (frame_number, batch_ts_us, repeated InputEnvelope). All tile_id/node_id as SceneId. All timestamps use _mono_us suffix.
- **Requirement: ContextMenu Dispatch (Pointer)** (lines 433-435, spec.md) — right-click mapped to ContextMenuEvent by event preprocessor, bypassing gesture recognizer.
- **Requirement: Headless Testability** (lines 422-424, spec.md) — all input behavior exercisable without display server or physical GPU; hit-test operates on pure Rust data structures.

## WHEN/THEN Acceptance Scenarios
- WHEN user presses left mouse button on HitRegionNode THEN PointerDownEvent includes tile_id, node_id, device_id, button=PRIMARY, node-local x/y, display-space x/y, modifiers, timestamp_mono_us, interaction_id (line 284)
- WHEN PointerDownEvent enters Stage 1 THEN local feedback complete within 1ms (Stages 1+2 combined p99), event routing to agent gRPC stream within 2ms of Stage 2 completion (line 310)
- WHEN PointerDownEvent hits node N1 in tile T1 leased by Agent A THEN event routed to Agent A's session stream (line 321)
- WHEN NAVIGATE_NEXT command arrives and focus is None THEN runtime advances focus to first focusable element (line 325)
- WHEN point query against 50 tiles THEN hit-test completes in < 100µs (line 269)
- WHEN click lands where chrome element and content tile overlap THEN chrome wins hit-test and content tile does NOT receive event (line 273)
- WHEN HitRegionNode has event_mask.pointer_move=false THEN PointerMoveEvent NOT dispatched to owning agent (line 254)
- WHEN HitRegionNode has interaction_id="submit-button" and receives ClickEvent THEN ClickEvent includes interaction_id="submit-button" (line 258)
- WHEN PointerDownEvent serialized THEN tile_id and node_id encoded as SceneId (16-byte LE UUIDv7) (line 373)
- WHEN user right-clicks on HitRegionNode with event_mask.context_menu=true THEN agent receives ContextMenuEvent (not GestureEvent) (line 439)
- WHEN Layer 0 test injects synthetic PointerDownEvent at (50,50) THEN hit-test returns correct result without GPU or display server (line 428)

## Latency budgets
- input_to_local_ack: < 4ms p99
- hit_test: < 100µs p99 (50 tiles)
- Stage 1 Input Drain: < 500µs p99
- Stage 2 Local Feedback: < 500µs p99
- Stage 1+2 combined: < 1ms p99
- Event dispatch to agent (hit-test + session lookup + serialize + enqueue): < 2ms from Stage 2 completion

## Test scenes
- input_highlight — pointer dispatch validation
- chatty_dashboard_touch — multi-tile pointer scenarios
- Budget assertions: test_input_to_local_ack_p99_within_budget(), test_hit_test_p99_within_budget()

## Crate/file location
crates/tze_hud_input/src/ (dispatch.rs, hit_test.rs, pointer.rs, events.rs)

## Depends on
- Sub-bead #1 (focus tree — click-to-focus before dispatch)
- Epic 2 (frame pipeline — compositor stages)
- Epic 1 (HitRegionNode, scene graph)
- Epic 3 (timestamp_mono_us from timing model)

## v1-mandatory
Pointer Event Types, Event Dispatch Flow, Event Routing Resolution, Hit-Test Performance, HitRegionNode Primitive, Event Dispatch to Agent Latency, Protobuf Schema for Input Events, ContextMenu Dispatch (Pointer), Headless Testability

## v1-reserved exclusions
- Gesture Recognition Latency (< 50µs per recognizer update, < 1ms total) — v1-reserved
- V1 Gesture Fallback — tap/click recognition MAY ship in v1, full pipeline deferred
- Full Gesture Pipeline (Tap, DoubleTap, LongPress, Drag, Pinch, Swipe + arbiter) — v1-reserved

## post-v1 exclusions
- Drag-and-Drop Protocol
- Stylus and Pressure Input (pressure, tilt, twist fields)

## Does NOT include
- Focus tree implementation (bead #1)
- Keyboard events or command input model (bead #3)
- Event batching and coalescing (bead #4)
- Pointer capture protocol (bead #5)
- Local feedback rendering (bead #6 — though dispatch pipeline feeds SceneLocalPatch to it)
title: Input: Pointer event dispatch pipeline

description:
Implement scene-graph-level reference counting and garbage collection for resources. Refcount tracks how many scene graph nodes reference each resource (regardless of owning agent). GC runs as compositor-thread epilogue in inter-frame idle window with a 5ms time budget per cycle.

## Parent epic
rig-tylk (Epic 10: Resource Store)

## Dependencies
- rig-etoq (RS-1: Content-addressed upload and deduplication)

## Spec requirements (resource-store/spec.md)

### Requirement: Scene-Graph-Level Reference Counting (lines 128-131, v1-mandatory)
Integer refcount per stored resource. Changes are atomic on compositor thread during mutation commit. Tracks scene graph node references regardless of owning agent. Newly uploaded resources start at refcount 0. Refcount never goes below zero (underflow is a bug — panic in debug, structured error in release).

### Requirement: Per-Agent Budget Accounting for Shared Resources (lines 151-153, v1-mandatory)
Texture bytes charged to agent whose node references the resource, not the uploader. Multiple agents referencing same resource: each charged full decoded size (per-agent double-counting). Prevents coordinated multi-agent budget bypass.

### Requirement: Garbage Collection Candidacy and Grace Period (lines 192-194, v1-mandatory)
Resources with refcount == 0 enter GC candidacy. Configurable grace period timer (default: 60s, min: 1s, max: 3600s) starts when refcount reaches zero. Eligible for deletion only after full grace period elapses at refcount == 0.

### Requirement: GC Cycle Timing (lines 207-209, v1-mandatory)
GC runs on configurable timer (default: 30s, min: 5s, max: 300s). Each cycle: scan candidates, evict those past grace period, free decoded data, remove from index. 5ms time budget per cycle; excess deferred to next cycle.

### Requirement: GC Frame Render Isolation (lines 218-220, v1-mandatory)
GC never runs during frame render. Runs as compositor-thread epilogue after GPU Submit + Present completes, in inter-frame idle window. Must not acquire locks held by render pipeline (stages 4-7). Must not touch GPU device state during render. Must not deallocate GPU textures bound to current frame's draw calls.

### Requirement: Resource Resurrection (lines 233-235, v1-mandatory)
GC candidate (refcount == 0, grace period not yet elapsed) is resurrectable. New node referencing candidate: refcount increments, resource transitions back to live, decoded in-memory form (if still resident) does not need reload.

### Requirement: Refcount Update Performance (lines 318-320, v1-mandatory)
Refcount update < 1 microsecond per operation. AtomicU32 in resource store index, single atomic increment/decrement during mutation commit, no lock contention.

### Requirement: Zero Post-Revocation Resource Footprint (lines 340-342, v1-mandatory)
After agent revocation and lease cleanup, all resources with refcount == 0 scheduled for GC within one GC cycle. After grace period, agent's resource footprint MUST be zero.

### Requirement: Budget Enforcement at Mutation Time (lines 351-353, v1-mandatory)
Budget checks for texture_bytes_per_tile and texture_bytes_total in mutation pipeline at per-mutation validation. All-or-nothing within mutation batch (atomic pipeline). Budget measured as decoded in-memory size, not raw upload size.

### Requirement: Font Asset Management (lines 255-258, v1-mandatory)
System and bundled fonts have permanent implicit holds, never GC'd. Font family resolution: (1) named variant from display profile, (2) custom ResourceId lookup with fallback to SystemSansSerif, (3) bundled default.

### Requirement: Font Cache (lines 270-272, v1-mandatory)
LRU cache bounded by configurable max (default: 64 MiB). Includes loaded font faces, shaped glyph caches, rasterized glyph atlases. Agent-uploaded fonts evicted LRU when budget exceeded. System/bundled fonts never evicted.

## WHEN/THEN acceptance scenarios

WHEN an agent creates a StaticImageNode referencing ResourceId X
THEN the refcount for X MUST increment by 1 [line 134-135]

WHEN a tile is deleted, cascading to all its nodes that reference resources
THEN the refcount for each referenced resource MUST decrement by 1 per deleted node [line 138-139]

WHEN Agent A and Agent B each create a node referencing the same ResourceId X, then Agent A deletes its node
THEN refcount MUST be 1 (Agent B's reference), and the resource MUST NOT be GC-eligible [line 142-143]

WHEN a bug would cause refcount to drop below zero
THEN the runtime MUST panic in debug builds and log a structured error in release builds [line 146-147]

WHEN Agent A (budget: 10 MiB) and Agent B (budget: 10 MiB) both reference a 4 MiB decoded texture
THEN Agent A MUST be charged 4 MiB and Agent B MUST be charged 4 MiB against their respective budgets [line 157-158]

WHEN an agent references a 500 KiB compressed PNG that decodes to 4 MiB RGBA8
THEN 4 MiB (decoded in-memory size) MUST be charged, not 500 KiB [line 161-162]

WHEN a resource's refcount reaches 0 and 30 seconds pass (less than 60s default grace)
THEN the resource MUST still be in the store and available for resurrection [line 198-199]

WHEN a resource's refcount has been 0 for at least 60 seconds and a GC cycle runs
THEN the resource MUST be evicted: decoded data freed, entry removed from store index [line 202-203]

WHEN a GC cycle has more eviction work than can be completed in 5ms
THEN remaining evictions MUST be deferred to the next GC cycle [line 213-214]

WHEN the compositor completes frame N's GPU submit and present
THEN GC MUST run in the inter-frame epilogue before stage 3 of frame N+1 begins [line 224-225]

WHEN a texture is still bound to the current frame's draw calls
THEN GC MUST NOT deallocate that texture; eviction deferred to next cycle [line 228-229]

WHEN a resource has refcount 0 for 20 seconds (within 60s grace) and a new node references it
THEN resource MUST be resurrected: refcount incremented to 1, no re-upload or re-decode needed [line 239-240]

WHEN a mutation commit increments or decrements a resource refcount
THEN the operation MUST complete within 1 microsecond [line 324-325]

WHEN Agent A is revoked, leases cleaned up, and all referenced resources reach refcount 0
THEN after grace period plus one GC cycle, Agent A's resource footprint MUST be exactly zero [line 346-347]

WHEN a mutation adds nodes to a single tile whose total decoded texture size would exceed texture_bytes_per_tile
THEN the entire mutation batch MUST be rejected with BUDGET_EXCEEDED_TEXTURE_BYTES [line 357-358]

WHEN no scene graph nodes reference a system font
THEN the font MUST remain available; system and bundled fonts have permanent implicit holds [line 261-262]

WHEN a TextMarkdownNode references a custom font ResourceId not in the store
THEN runtime MUST fall back to SystemSansSerif without notifying the agent [line 265-266]

WHEN the font cache exceeds 64 MiB
THEN the least recently used agent-uploaded font data MUST be evicted first; system/bundled fonts MUST NOT be evicted [line 276-277]

## Relevant test scenes
Refcount lifecycle scenes, GC timing scenes, budget enforcement scenes, font fallback scenes from the validation-framework's 25-scene registry

## Crate/file location
crates/tze_hud_resource/ — refcount.rs, gc.rs, budget.rs, font_cache.rs

## Wire vs debug format
ResourceId is raw 32 bytes on wire, hex for logging. Refcount is internal AtomicU32 — not exposed on wire.

## What this bead does NOT include
- Upload protocol and deduplication (bead #1)
- Cross-agent sharing semantics beyond refcount mechanics (bead #3)
- V1 ephemerality enforcement (bead #4)
- GPU texture compression BC7/ASTC (post-v1, spec lines 362-365)
- Persistent resource store (post-v1, spec lines 373-376)

## v1-mandatory
All requirements listed above.

## v1-reserved exclusions
None for this bead.

## post-v1 exclusions
- GPU Texture Compression BC7/ASTC (spec lines 362-365)
- Persistent Resource Store (spec lines 373-376)

close_reason: PR #118 merged after PR review - scene hierarchy implementation complete
description:
Implement the four-level scene hierarchy per scene-graph/spec.md, including Tab/Tile CRUD, node types, namespace isolation, and structural invariants.

## Spec References

### Requirement: Scene Graph Hierarchy (scene-graph/spec.md line 45)
Scene -> Tab[] -> Tile[] -> Node[]. Scene has 0-256 Tabs. Tab has 0-1024 Tiles. Tile has 0-64 Nodes arranged as an acyclic tree. No TabId, TileId, or NodeId may appear more than once.
Source: RFC 0001 §2.1 | Scope: v1-mandatory

### Requirement: Tab CRUD Operations (scene-graph/spec.md line 66)
CreateTab, DeleteTab, RenameTab, ReorderTab, SwitchActiveTab. Tab name: non-empty, max 128 UTF-8 bytes. Display_order unique across all tabs. Requires manage_tabs capability.
Source: RFC 0001 §2.2, §3.1, §3.3 | Scope: v1-mandatory

### Requirement: Tile CRUD Operations (scene-graph/spec.md line 87)
CreateTile, UpdateTileBounds, UpdateTileZOrder, UpdateTileOpacity, UpdateTileInputMode, UpdateTileSyncGroup, UpdateTileExpiry, DeleteTile. Requires valid lease on tile's namespace. CreateTile requires create_tiles capability. All tile mutations require modify_own_tiles capability.
Source: RFC 0001 §2.3, §3.1, §3.3 | Scope: v1-mandatory

### Requirement: Tile Field Invariants (scene-graph/spec.md line 104)
Opacity in [0.0, 1.0]. Bounds width/height > 0.0. Bounds fully within tab display area. Agent-owned tiles z_order < ZONE_TILE_Z_MIN (0x8000_0000). resource_budget.max_nodes in [1, 64].
Source: RFC 0001 §2.3 | Scope: v1-mandatory

### Requirement: V1 Node Types (scene-graph/spec.md line 125)
Four node types: SolidColorNode (color, bounds), TextMarkdownNode (CommonMark up to 65535 UTF-8 bytes, font_size_px > 0.0, font family, color, alignment, overflow), StaticImageNode (ResourceId, bounds, fit mode), HitRegionNode (bounds, interaction_id, accepts_focus, accepts_pointer). Each node has a SceneId and ordered list of child SceneIds.
Source: RFC 0001 §2.4 | Scope: v1-mandatory

### Requirement: Namespace Isolation (scene-graph/spec.md line 32)
Every agent session assigned a namespace. TileId belongs to exactly one namespace. Agents MUST NOT reference tiles they do not own. NodeIds unique scene-globally.
Source: RFC 0001 §1.2 | Scope: v1-mandatory

### Requirement: Struct Overhead Budgets (scene-graph/spec.md line 302)
Per-tile struct < 200 bytes (excluding texture data and nodes). Per-node struct < 150 bytes (excluding content payloads). At max capacity (64 nodes/tile), total structural overhead ~9.8 KB.
Source: RFC 0001 §8, §10 | Scope: v1-mandatory

### Requirement: Ephemeral Scene State (scene-graph/spec.md line 324)
Scene graph, leases, zone publishes, sessions, hit-region state, telemetry are ephemeral (lost on restart). Agents must re-establish sessions after restart.
Source: RFC 0001 §6 | Scope: v1-mandatory

## WHEN/THEN Scenarios from Spec

- Scenario: Tab limit enforcement (line 50)
  WHEN an agent attempts CreateTab and 256 tabs already exist
  THEN the runtime MUST reject with BudgetExceeded

- Scenario: Tile limit enforcement (line 54)
  WHEN an agent attempts CreateTile on a tab with 1024 tiles
  THEN the runtime MUST reject with BudgetExceeded

- Scenario: Node limit enforcement (line 58)
  WHEN an agent attempts InsertNode on a tile with 64 nodes
  THEN the runtime MUST reject with NodeCountExceeded

- Scenario: Duplicate ID rejection (line 62)
  WHEN an agent submits CreateTile with a TileId that already exists
  THEN the runtime MUST reject the batch with DuplicateId

- Scenario: Create and switch tab (line 71)
  WHEN an agent with manage_tabs capability submits CreateTab followed by SwitchActiveTab
  THEN the new tab MUST be created and become the active tab

- Scenario: Tab rename (line 75)
  WHEN an agent submits RenameTab with a new name of 100 UTF-8 bytes
  THEN the tab name MUST be updated

- Scenario: Tab name too long (line 79)
  WHEN an agent submits CreateTab with a name exceeding 128 UTF-8 bytes
  THEN the runtime MUST reject with InvalidFieldValue

- Scenario: Tab mutation without capability (line 83)
  WHEN an agent without manage_tabs capability submits CreateTab
  THEN the runtime MUST reject with CapabilityMissing

- Scenario: Create tile with valid lease (line 92)
  WHEN an agent with create_tiles and modify_own_tiles capabilities and a valid lease submits CreateTile
  THEN the tile MUST be created with specified bounds, z_order, and opacity

- Scenario: Tile mutation with expired lease (line 96)
  WHEN an agent submits UpdateTileBounds but the tile's lease has expired
  THEN the runtime MUST reject with LeaseExpired

- Scenario: Delete tile (line 100)
  WHEN an agent submits DeleteTile for a tile it owns with a valid lease
  THEN the tile and all its nodes MUST be removed

- Scenario: Opacity out of range (line 109)
  WHEN an agent submits UpdateTileOpacity with opacity = 1.5
  THEN the runtime MUST reject with InvalidFieldValue

- Scenario: Zero-size bounds (line 113)
  WHEN an agent submits CreateTile with width = 0.0
  THEN the runtime MUST reject with BoundsOutOfRange

- Scenario: Bounds outside tab area (line 117)
  WHEN UpdateTileBounds with x + width exceeding tab display width
  THEN reject with BoundsOutOfRange

- Scenario: Z-order in reserved zone band (line 121)
  WHEN CreateTile with z_order = 0x8000_0000
  THEN reject with InvalidFieldValue (z_order >= ZONE_TILE_Z_MIN is reserved)

- Scenario: TextMarkdownNode content limit (line 130)
  WHEN TextMarkdownNode with content exceeding 65535 UTF-8 bytes
  THEN reject with InvalidFieldValue

- Scenario: StaticImageNode with unknown resource (line 134)
  WHEN StaticImageNode referencing unknown ResourceId
  THEN reject with ResourceNotFound

- Scenario: Cross-namespace tile access denied (line 37)
  WHEN agent "weather-agent" (namespace "wtr") attempts to mutate a tile owned by namespace "cal"
  THEN reject with CapabilityMissing or LeaseNotFound

- Scenario: Resource sharing across namespaces (line 41)
  WHEN agent "cal" references a ResourceId uploaded by agent "wtr"
  THEN the runtime MUST allow read access (default sharing policy: read-allowed, write-disallowed)

- Scenario: Tile struct size (line 307)
  WHEN size_of::<Tile>() plus metadata allocation is measured
  THEN it MUST be less than 200 bytes

- Scenario: Node struct size (line 311)
  WHEN size_of::<Node>() plus ID allocation is measured
  THEN it MUST be less than 150 bytes

- Scenario: Scene lost on restart (line 329)
  WHEN the runtime process restarts
  THEN the scene graph MUST be empty; agents MUST reconnect and re-create

## Acceptance Criteria

- assert_layer0_invariants() passes for all 25 test scenes from Epic 0
- Namespace isolation property tests pass (proptest from Epic 0): random mutations across namespaces never cross boundaries
- Tab/Tile/Node CRUD scenarios from spec all pass as unit tests
- Budget limits enforced: 256 tabs, 1024 tiles/tab, 64 nodes/tile
- Struct size budgets: Tile < 200 bytes, Node < 150 bytes (measured with std::mem::size_of plus allocations)
- All V1 node types constructable and validatable without GPU context

## Epic 0 Test Gates

- assert_layer0_invariants() on all 25 test scenes (empty_scene through policy_arbitration_collision)
- Namespace isolation proptest suite
- Test scenes most relevant: single_tile_solid, three_tiles_no_overlap, overlapping_tiles_zorder, max_tiles_stress, three_agents_contention, tab_switch

## Crate/File Location

- Primary: crates/tze_hud_scene/src/graph.rs (SceneGraph, Tab, Tile, Node types)
- Types: crates/tze_hud_scene/src/types.rs (node data types, Rect, Rgba, capabilities)
- Validation: crates/tze_hud_scene/src/validation.rs (field invariant checks)
- Tests: crates/tze_hud_scene/src/ (unit tests) and crates/tze_hud_scene/tests/ (integration)

## v1 Scope Tags

- v1-mandatory: Scene Graph Hierarchy (line 45), Tab CRUD (line 66), Tile CRUD (line 87), Tile Field Invariants (line 104), V1 Node Types (line 125), Namespace Isolation (line 32), Struct Overhead Budgets (line 302), Ephemeral Scene State (line 324)
- v1-reserved: Mobile profile differences (config-driven, not separate hierarchy code)
- post-v1: Additional node types beyond the v1 four, persistent scene state

## What This Does NOT Include

- Atomic batch mutation pipeline (bead #3) — this bead defines individual CRUD operations, not batch atomicity
- Zone ontology (bead #4) — zone types, instances, publications are separate
- Hit-test spatial query algorithm (bead #5) — HitRegionNode is defined as a node type here, but the traversal algorithm is bead #5
- Scene snapshot serialization (bead #6)
- Lease governance state machine (separate epic) — this bead checks lease validity but does not implement the lease lifecycle
title: Scene hierarchy: Scene, Tab, Tile, Node four-level tree

description:
mutation::TimingHints uses plain u64 for present_at_wall_us and expires_at_wall_us. The codebase convention requires _wall_us fields to use WallUs newtypes (timing/domains.rs) for domain safety.

Additionally, the struct name TimingHints collides with timing::TimingHints in the public API, requiring it to be re-exported as MutationTimingHints in lib.rs.

Fix: rename to BatchTimingHints and change field types to Option<WallUs> to enforce domain safety and eliminate the name collision.

Discovered from rig-pkcv PR review (PR #127, thread PRRT_kwDORtExfM52UYHb).

close_reason: PR #122 merged after PR review: TTL timer, auto-renewal, and safe-mode suspension implementation complete
description:
Implement lease lifecycle timing per lease-governance/spec.md Requirement: Auto-Renewal Policy (lines 71-91), Requirement: Safe Mode Suspends Leases (lines 92-99), Requirement: Safe Mode Resume (lines 105-108), Requirement: Max Suspension Time (lines 114-122), Requirement: Suspension Preserves State (lines 123-131), and Requirement: TTL Accounting Precision (lines 289-296).

## Auto-Renewal Policies (spec lines 71-91, Requirement: Auto-Renewal Policy)
Three renewal policies:
- MANUAL (default): agent must explicitly send LeaseRequest with operation = RENEW before TTL expires
- AUTO_RENEW: runtime auto-renews at 75% TTL elapsed if session is Active and no budget violations pending; agent receives LeaseResponse with result = GRANTED on each auto-renewal
- ONE_SHOT: expires at TTL without renewal option; agent must request a new lease

Auto-renewal disabled (timer not armed) when:
1. Agent enters budget warning state (resumes if warning clears before TTL expires)
2. Session enters Disconnecting state
3. Safe mode is entered (TTL clock paused; timer resumes with TTL clock on safe mode exit)

ONE_SHOT leases: not subject to auto-renewal, but TTL clock IS paused during suspension.

## Safe Mode Suspends Leases (spec lines 92-99, Requirement: Safe Mode Suspends Leases)
On safe mode entry:
- All ACTIVE leases transition to SUSPENDED (NOT REVOKED)
- Agent sessions receive SessionSuspended with reason = "viewer_safe_mode"
- LeaseSuspend sent for each lease
- Mutations rejected with SAFE_MODE_ACTIVE
- TTL clock paused
- Auto-renewal suspended
- Suspension completes within 1 frame (16.6ms)

## Safe Mode Resume (spec lines 105-108, Requirement: Safe Mode Resume)
On safe mode exit:
- All SUSPENDED leases transition back to ACTIVE
- Agents receive SessionResumed
- LeaseResume sent with adjusted_expires_at_wall_us and suspension_duration_us
- Staleness badges clear within 1 frame
- Tiles render from last-committed scene state
- Resume completes within 2 frames (33.2ms)
- Agent does NOT re-request a lease

## Max Suspension Time (spec lines 114-122, Requirement: Max Suspension Time)
A lease MUST NOT remain SUSPENDED indefinitely. After max_suspension_time_ms (default: 300,000ms / 5 minutes), lease transitions to REVOKED. If safe mode remains active beyond this duration, suspended leases are progressively revoked (oldest first).

## Suspension Preserves State (spec lines 123-131, Requirement: Suspension Preserves State)
When suspended: all tiles, node trees, zone publications, resource allocations, and lease metadata preserved in memory. No resources freed.

## TTL Accounting Precision (spec lines 289-296, Requirement: TTL Accounting Precision)
TTL accounting during suspension accurate to +/- 100ms. Effective lease expiry: granted_at_wall_us + (ttl_ms * 1000) + suspension_duration_us.

## WHEN/THEN Scenarios from Spec

Scenario: Auto-renewal at 75% TTL (spec lines 76-78)
- WHEN a lease with AUTO_RENEW policy reaches 75% of its TTL
- THEN the runtime automatically renews the lease and sends LeaseResponse with result = GRANTED

Scenario: Auto-renewal disabled during budget warning (spec lines 80-82)
- WHEN a lease with AUTO_RENEW policy enters budget warning state
- THEN the auto-renewal timer is disarmed; the lease continues aging toward expiry and the agent must renew manually or resolve the budget warning before TTL elapses

Scenario: ONE_SHOT lease expires (spec lines 84-86)
- WHEN a ONE_SHOT lease reaches its TTL
- THEN the lease transitions to EXPIRED without renewal option

Scenario: ONE_SHOT lease suspended during safe mode (spec lines 88-91)
- WHEN a ONE_SHOT lease is active when safe mode is entered
- THEN the TTL clock pauses during suspension; on safe mode exit the lease transitions back to ACTIVE with adjusted expiry and can serve its full remaining TTL

Scenario: Safe mode suspends all leases (spec lines 97-99)
- WHEN the viewer triggers safe mode
- THEN all ACTIVE leases transition to SUSPENDED within one frame, agents receive SessionSuspended, and mutations are rejected

Scenario: TTL paused during suspension (spec lines 101-103)
- WHEN a lease is SUSPENDED for 2 minutes and then resumed
- THEN the effective TTL does not count the 2 minutes of suspension time

Scenario: Resume preserves lease identity (spec lines 110-112)
- WHEN safe mode is exited
- THEN all previously SUSPENDED leases return to ACTIVE with the same LeaseId, capability scope, and budget

Scenario: Suspension timeout (spec lines 119-121)
- WHEN a lease is SUSPENDED for more than 5 minutes (default)
- THEN the lease transitions to REVOKED with revoke_reason = SUSPENSION_TIMEOUT

Scenario: Tile content preserved during suspension (spec lines 128-130)
- WHEN a lease transitions from ACTIVE to SUSPENDED and back to ACTIVE
- THEN all tile content, node trees, and geometry remain intact without agent re-submission

Scenario: TTL correctly adjusted after suspension (spec lines 294-296)
- WHEN a lease with ttl_ms = 60000 is suspended for 10,000ms and then resumed
- THEN the effective expiry is extended by 10,000ms (within +/- 100ms accuracy)

## State Machine Diagram Reference
Safe mode transitions from lease-governance/spec.md lines 92-108 (ACTIVE->SUSPENDED->ACTIVE).
Max suspension: SUSPENDED->REVOKED (spec lines 114-122).

## Acceptance Criteria
- TTL countdown verified with TestClock
- Suspension pauses TTL correctly (within +/- 100ms)
- Auto-renewal fires at 75% TTL for AUTO_RENEW leases
- Auto-renewal disabled during budget warning, disconnecting, safe mode
- ONE_SHOT leases expire without renewal
- ONE_SHOT lease TTL paused during suspension
- Safe mode suspends all ACTIVE leases within 1 frame
- Resume restores all SUSPENDED leases within 2 frames
- Max suspension time triggers REVOKED (oldest first)
- Suspension preserves all tile/node/zone state in memory
- adjusted_expires_at_wall_us correctly calculated on resume
- LeaseStateMachine trait contract tests from Epic 0 (rig-ho4b) pass for TTL scenarios

## Crate/File Location
crates/tze_hud_scene/src/lease/ttl.rs (TTL timer, renewal logic), crates/tze_hud_scene/src/lease/suspension.rs (safe mode suspend/resume, state preservation).

## Relevant Test Scenes
- lease_expiry (validation-framework 25-scene registry)

## v1-mandatory Requirements Implemented
- Requirement: Auto-Renewal Policy (spec lines 71-91)
- Requirement: Safe Mode Suspends Leases (spec lines 92-99)
- Requirement: Safe Mode Resume (spec lines 105-108)
- Requirement: Max Suspension Time (spec lines 114-122)
- Requirement: Suspension Preserves State (spec lines 123-131)
- Requirement: TTL Accounting Precision (spec lines 289-296)

## v1-reserved exclusions
- Budget Renegotiation (spec lines 317-319): cannot change TTL/budget mid-lease

## post-v1 exclusions
- None specific to this bead

## What This Does NOT Include
- Core state machine transitions (bead #1)
- Disconnect grace / orphan handling timers (bead #3)
- Priority or capability scope (bead #4)
- Resource budget enforcement or three-tier ladder (bead #5)
title: TTL, renewal, and expiration — lease lifecycle timing

description: RFC 0002 §2.4 specifies the MCP bridge as a JSON-RPC endpoint. No MCP code exists anywhere in the repo. Implement tze_hud_mcp crate with: (1) MCP server over stdio or Streamable HTTP, (2) tool set: create_tab, create_tile, set_content, dismiss, list_scene, publish_to_zone, list_zones, (3) translation layer from MCP JSON-RPC to internal scene mutations, (4) structured error responses per architecture.md error model. This is required for the v1 success criterion: 'An LLM with only MCP access can publish a subtitle to a zone with one tool call.'
title: Implement MCP compatibility bridge (create_tab, create_tile, set_content, publish_to_zone, list_zones)

close_reason: PR #141 merged after PR review. Shell #5 disconnection badges and backpressure signals implementation reviewed, fixes applied, and landed on agent/rig-pt5k.
description:
# Disconnection Badges and Backpressure Signals

Implement status indicator overlays for disconnected agents and agents under queue pressure: disconnection badges on tiles of disconnected agents, budget warning badges, and generic backpressure visual signals.

## Ownership rule
The shell is the SOLE owner of badge rendering in the chrome layer. Badge state is written to ChromeState by the control plane (lease manager, session manager) and read by the chrome render pass. Agents are intentionally NOT told the scene is frozen — backpressure signals are generic and apply to any queue-pressure scenario.

## Spec references (system-shell/spec.md)

### Requirement: Disconnection Badge (line 171)
When agent's lease enters orphaned state (agent disconnected, within grace period), disconnection badge appears on all affected tiles within one frame. Badge: dim plug/link-break icon at 70% opacity (static in v1, no animation). Tile content renders at reduced opacity (70%). Badge clears immediately when agent reconnects and reclaims lease.
- Scenario: Disconnection badge appears (line 176) — WHEN agent disconnects and lease enters grace period THEN dim link-break icon appears on all affected tiles within one frame
- Scenario: Disconnection badge clears on reconnect (line 180) — WHEN agent reconnects and reclaims lease THEN disconnection badge removed immediately

### Requirement: Budget Warning Badge (line 197)
When agent's resource consumption reaches 80% of session budget, budget warning badge appears as subtle amber border highlight (2px, 70% opacity) on affected tiles. Badge removed when consumption drops below 80%.
- Scenario: Budget warning badge appears (line 202) — WHEN agent's texture memory usage reaches 80% of budget THEN amber border highlight appears on all tiles under that agent's lease

### Requirement: Freeze Backpressure Signal (line 158)
(Cross-reference with bead #3) Agents NOT informed scene is frozen. Generic visual signal when agent's queue is under pressure. Intentionally generic to avoid leaking viewer state (freeze vs genuine load).
- Scenario: Queue pressure signal (line 163) — WHEN per-session freeze queue reaches 80% capacity THEN runtime sends MUTATION_QUEUE_PRESSURE in MutationResult
- Scenario: Mutation dropped signal (line 167) — WHEN queue full and state-stream mutation evicted THEN runtime sends MUTATION_DROPPED

### Requirement: Override Control Guarantees (line 206)
(Cross-reference with bead #1) Badge appearance/removal is frame-bounded: visual effect within one frame (<= 16.6ms).
- Scenario: Override is frame-bounded (line 211) — WHEN viewer triggers dismiss THEN tile disappears within one frame

## Backpressure signal behavior (from prompt)
- Backpressure: generic visual signal when agent's queue is under pressure
- Agents not told the scene is frozen (intentionally generic signals)
- Signal applies to any queue-pressure scenario, not specifically freeze

## Acceptance criteria
- Disconnection badge (dim link-break icon, 70% opacity) appears on tiles of disconnected agent within 1 frame of disconnect detection
- Tile content renders at reduced opacity (70%) while badge active
- Badge clears within 1 frame of reconnection
- Budget warning badge (amber border highlight, 2px, 70% opacity) appears at 80% budget usage
- Budget warning badge clears when consumption drops below 80%
- Backpressure visual signal visible under queue load (generic, not freeze-specific)
- Timing assertions: badge appear/clear within 1 frame (16.6ms)
- Badge rendering reads from ChromeState only (no agent state dependency)
- Static badges in v1 (no animation)

## v1-mandatory requirements
- Disconnection Badge (line 171)
- Budget Warning Badge (line 197)
- Freeze Backpressure Signal (line 158) — visual signal aspect; queue logic in bead #3

## v1-reserved exclusions
None for this bead.

## post-v1 exclusions
- Animated badges (v1 is static only)
- Badge animations/transitions beyond appear/clear

## Crate/file location
crates/tze_hud_runtime/src/shell/badges.rs — disconnection badge rendering, budget warning badge rendering, backpressure visual signal, badge state in ChromeState

## What this does NOT include
- Chrome layer rendering framework (bead #1)
- Safe mode state machine (bead #2)
- Freeze queue and backpressure message logic (bead #3) — this bead renders the visual signal; bead #3 implements the queue and sends MUTATION_QUEUE_PRESSURE/MUTATION_DROPPED
- Privacy redaction (bead #4)
- Lease orphan detection and grace period (Epic 4)
- Resource budget enforcement and 80% threshold detection (Epic 4)

close_reason: PR #112 merged after PR review. Seven-level arbitration stack implementation complete with all spec compliance bugs fixed.
description:
# Seven-Level Arbitration Stack

Implement the fixed 7-level arbitration stack per policy-arbitration/spec.md.

## Spec Requirements

### Requirement: Seven-Level Arbitration Stack (spec lines 10-17)
Fixed 7-level precedence (highest to lowest):
- Level 0: Human Override
- Level 1: Safety
- Level 2: Privacy
- Level 3: Security
- Level 4: Attention
- Level 5: Resource
- Level 6: Content

Ordering is doctrine and MUST NOT be modified.

### Requirement: Cross-Level Conflict Resolution (spec lines 19-46)
- Rule CL-1: higher level MUST always win
- Rule CL-2: side effects of losing level MUST be suppressed, not deferred
- Rule CL-3: winning level's override type MUST apply
- Short-circuit: lower levels MUST NOT be evaluated when a higher level rejects

### Requirement: ArbitrationOutcome Types (spec lines 229-241)
Six outcomes: Commit, CommitRedacted, Queue, Reject, Shed, Blocked.

### Requirement: Override Type Taxonomy (spec lines 242-253)
- Level 0: Suppress/Redirect/Block
- Level 1: Suppress/Redirect
- Level 2: Transform
- Level 3: Suppress
- Level 4: Block
- Level 5: Suppress/Transform
- Level 6: Suppress

### Requirement: Override Composition (spec lines 255-267)
Highest level's override type takes precedence. Transform and Block can compose (queued-with-redaction).

### Requirement: Within-Level Conflict Resolution (spec lines 342-354)
Level-specific tie-breaking: L0 input-event order, L1 severity, L2 most-restrictive, L3 conjunctive, L4 longer deferral, L5 frame-time guardian precedence, L6 zone's ContentionPolicy.

### Requirement: Freeze Semantics at Level 0 (spec lines 268-279)
Freeze is Level 0. During freeze: mutations queued (not rejected), resource budgets paused, degradation ladder paused, attention deferred, rendering frozen at last committed state, override controls remain active, agent input routing suspended. Configurable max freeze duration with auto-unfreeze.

## WHEN/THEN Scenarios (from spec)

- WHEN the arbitration stack is initialized THEN it contains exactly 7 levels numbered 0-6 with Human Override at 0 and Content at 6 (spec line 16-17)
- WHEN Level 2 (Privacy) says "redact tile" but Level 6 (Content) says "show tile" THEN the tile is redacted and content contention result is irrelevant (spec lines 24-26)
- WHEN Level 0 (Human) freezes the scene but Level 5 (Resource) would shed a tile THEN the tile stays frozen and is not shed (spec lines 28-30)
- WHEN Level 3 (Security) denies a capability THEN Level 5 (Resource) and Level 6 (Content) are never evaluated for that mutation (spec lines 32-34)
- WHEN Level 1 (Safety) enters safe mode while Level 4 (Attention) has queued notifications THEN the queued notifications MUST be discarded (spec lines 36-38)
- WHEN Level 2 (Privacy) would redact AND Level 4 (Attention) would queue for quiet hours THEN mutation is queued-with-redaction, not suppressed (spec lines 40-42)
- WHEN the viewer dismisses a tile with a valid ACTIVE lease THEN the lease is revoked immediately (Level 0 wins); agent receives LeaseResponse REVOKED with revoke_reason=VIEWER_DISMISSED (spec lines 44-46)
- WHEN CommitRedacted outcome THEN mutation committed but rendering uses redaction placeholder (spec lines 234-236)
- WHEN scene frozen and agent submits mutation THEN outcome is Blocked with block_reason=Freeze, mutation queued (spec lines 238-240)
- WHEN Level 0 blocks (freeze) and Level 5 would suppress (budget) THEN mutation is blocked (freeze wins) (spec lines 260-262)
- WHEN Level 2 redacts and Level 4 queues for quiet hours THEN mutation queued with redaction flag, rendered with placeholder when delivered (spec lines 264-266)
- WHEN scene frozen and frame-time would trigger degradation THEN degradation ladder does not advance (spec lines 274-275)
- WHEN scene frozen longer than max freeze duration THEN auto-unfreeze with DegradationNotice advisory (spec lines 277-279)
- WHEN two viewers with different access levels present THEN most restrictive applies (spec lines 347-348)
- WHEN mutation requires both create_tiles and publish_zone:subtitle THEN both must pass; failure of either rejects (spec lines 351-353)

## Test Scenes
- policy_matrix_basic: tiles visible/hidden based on policy evaluation result
- policy_arbitration_collision: multi-level collision end-to-end

## Acceptance Criteria
- PolicyEvaluator trait tests from Epic 0 (rig-ge28) pass
- Stack ordering enforced (levels 0-6 exactly, immutable)
- Short-circuit behavior verified (higher level reject stops evaluation)
- Override composition verified (Transform+Block composes)
- Within-level tie-breaking implemented per level-specific rules
- ArbitrationOutcome type enum covers all 6 variants
- Override Type Taxonomy matches spec exactly

## Purity Constraint
Policy evaluation MUST be a pure function over a typed PolicyContext — no side effects. Freeze/safe-mode state transitions are read-only inputs; shell owns the state machine (spec lines 364-366).

## Crate/File Location
crates/tze_hud_policy/src/stack.rs — ArbitrationStack, ArbitrationOutcome, OverrideType
crates/tze_hud_policy/src/lib.rs — public API re-exports

## v1-mandatory requirements
Seven-Level Arbitration Stack, Cross-Level Conflict Resolution, ArbitrationOutcome Types, Override Type Taxonomy, Override Composition, Within-Level Conflict Resolution, Freeze Semantics at Level 0

## v1-reserved exclusions
- Per-Agent Arbitration Diagnostics RPC (spec lines 376-383)

## post-v1 exclusions
- Dynamic Policy Rules (spec lines 367-374)
- Freeze/safe-mode state transitions (owned by system-shell)

## What this does NOT include
- Per-mutation evaluation pipeline (bead #2)
- Per-event / per-frame evaluation pipelines (bead #3)
- Attention budget / quiet hours implementation (bead #4)
- Shell state machine for freeze/safe-mode (Epic 11)

description:
End-to-end test: three resident agents connect via gRPC, acquire leases, publish to zones, create tiles, and coexist without interference.

## Test scenario
- Agent A: weather dashboard (create tiles, update content at 1Hz)
- Agent B: notification agent (publish to notification zone, stack contention)
- Agent C: media agent (publish to subtitle zone, latest-wins contention)
- Verify: namespace isolation, lease priority shedding, zone contention resolution, no cross-agent content leakage

## Spec references
- scene-graph/spec.md — namespace isolation (agents cannot access each other's tiles)
- lease-governance/spec.md — priority-based shedding under contention, lease TTL enforcement
- scene-events/spec.md — subscription filtering (agents receive only their own relevant events)
- validation-framework/spec.md — Requirement: V1 Success Criterion - Live Multi-Agent Presence (line 313-320): "3 LLM agents simultaneously holding tiles on a screen, updating content in real time, governed by leases, at 60fps"
- validation-framework/spec.md — Requirement: V1 Success Criterion - Security Isolation (line 335-343): "prevent a rogue agent from affecting other agents"
- validation-framework/spec.md — Requirement: Test Scene Registry (line 160-172): three_agents_contention scene

## Cross-epic references
- Validates Epic 1 (scene graph): namespace isolation, tile CRUD
- Validates Epic 4 (lease governance): priority shedding, concurrent leases
- Validates Epic 6 (session protocol): multi-agent gRPC connections
- Validates Epic 9 (zone system): zone contention resolution policies

## Acceptance criteria
- three_agents_contention test scene passes end-to-end
- All three agents render independently with correct layout and z-order
- Zone contention resolves per policy (stack for notifications, latest-wins for subtitles)
- Namespace isolation holds: no agent can read/write another's tiles
- Compositor renders at 60fps with all three agents active
- Lease priority shedding works: lower-priority agent's lease is shed under resource pressure

## Test scene registry
- three_agents_contention (primary)
- zone_conflict_two_publishers (zone contention validation)
- zone_publish_subtitle (subtitle zone agent)

## Artifact expectations
- Test produces: per-agent tile ownership map, zone contention resolution log, namespace isolation verification report, compositor frame rate measurements (JSON)

## V1 success criteria mapping
- Thesis 1: An LLM can hold a tile and have it render at 60fps (each agent holds tiles at 60fps)
- Thesis 2: The lease model works (concurrent leases with TTL and priority)
- Thesis 3: Multiple agents coexist without interference (3 agents verified)
- Thesis 6: Zones work as LLM-first surface (agents publish to zones)

## Crate/file location
- tze_hud/tests/integration/multi_agent.rs (integration test)
- tze_hud/tests/scenes/ (test scene definitions)

## V1 scope tags
- v1-mandatory: Multi-agent coexistence, namespace isolation, zone contention resolution, lease priority
- v1-reserved: None
- post-v1: Dynamic orchestrator agent, inter-agent event bus

## Scope boundary — what this bead does NOT include
- Does NOT implement agent authentication (that is Epic 5)
- Does NOT implement the zone system itself (that is Epic 9)
- Does NOT implement the scene graph (that is Epic 1)
- This bead only exercises cross-subsystem integration of already-implemented features
title: E12.1: Multi-agent integration test

close_reason: PR #121 merged after PR review. Per-mutation evaluation pipeline implementation complete.
description:
# Per-Mutation Evaluation Pipeline

Implement per-mutation policy checks per policy-arbitration/spec.md.

## Spec Requirements

### Requirement: Per-Mutation Evaluation Pipeline (spec lines 216-227)
Every mutation in every MutationBatch MUST pass through per-mutation evaluation:
- Tile mutations: Security (3) -> Resource (5) -> Content (6)
- Zone publications: Override preemption (0) -> Security (3) -> Privacy (2, redaction decoration) -> Attention (4) -> Resource (5) -> Content (6)
- Short-circuit: if a higher level rejects, lower levels MUST NOT be evaluated
- Per-mutation policy check MUST complete in < 50us

### Requirement: Level 3 Security Enforcement (spec lines 127-141)
- Enforce capability scopes, lease validity, namespace isolation
- All required capabilities MUST pass (conjunctive)
- Capability checks MUST be hash-table lookups; each < 5us
- Produce Reject(CapabilityDenied), Reject(CapabilityScopeInsufficient), or Reject(NamespaceViolation) with missing capability named in hint field

### Requirement: Capability Registry Canonical Names (spec lines 281-292)
- snake_case naming convention
- Canonical names per RFC 0009 §8.1 AS AMENDED by RFC 0005 Round 14: read_scene_topology (not read_scene), access_input_events (not receive_input), publish_zone:<zone> (not zone_publish:<zone>)
- Colon separation for sub-scoped identifiers
- Only publish_zone:* wildcard supported
- Pre-Round-14 names (read_scene, receive_input, zone_publish) MUST be treated as superseded and MUST NOT be used

### Requirement: Level 2 Privacy Evaluation (spec lines 91-106)
- Compare tile VisibilityClassification against current ViewerClass
- Access matrix: Owner sees all; HouseholdMember sees public+household; KnownGuest/Unknown/Nobody see only public
- Multi-viewer: most restrictive applies
- Privacy transitions < 2 frames (33.2ms)

### Requirement: Privacy Zone Ceiling Rule (spec lines 108-115)
- Effective classification = max(agent_declared, zone_default)
- Agent cannot escalate visibility beyond zone ceiling

### Requirement: Redaction Ownership (spec lines 117-124)
- Level 2 owns ALL redaction decisions
- [privacy] config is single source of truth for redaction_style
- ChromeConfig MUST NOT contain redaction_style
- Chrome renders redaction visual but does not decide what to redact

### Requirement: Level 5 Resource Enforcement (spec lines 168-179)
- Enforce per-agent budgets and degradation ladder
- Over-budget batches rejected atomically
- Degradation shedding does NOT produce error to agent
- Zone state MUST be updated for shed mutations (scene state correct, render omitted)
- Transactional mutations (CreateTile, DeleteTile, LeaseRequest, LeaseRelease) MUST never be shed

### Requirement: Level 6 Content Resolution (spec lines 181-192)
- Resolve zone contention using ContentionPolicy: LatestWins, Stack, MergeByKey, Replace
- Same-frame contention resolved in arrival order
- Cross-tab zone isolation enforced
- Zone contention resolution < 20us

### Requirement: Degradation Does Not Bypass Arbitration (spec lines 355-362)
- Shed mutation at Level 5 MUST have already passed all higher levels
- Zone state updated even for shed mutations

### Requirement: Policy Evaluation Latency Budgets (spec lines 294-312)
- Per-mutation policy check < 50us
- Capability lookup < 5us
- Zone contention resolution < 20us

### Requirement: Policy Telemetry (spec lines 315-322)
- PolicyTelemetry struct: per_frame_eval_us, per_mutation_eval_us_p99, mutations_rejected, mutations_redacted, mutations_queued, mutations_shed, override_commands_processed

### Requirement: Arbitration Telemetry Events (spec lines 324-331)
- Every Level 3 rejection and Level 5 shed: telemetry record with level, error code, agent ID, mutation ref, timestamp
- Levels 2/4: one record per session per minute when active

### Requirement: Capability Grant Audit (spec lines 333-340)
- Every grant/revocation logged with agent ID, capability name, timestamp, granting source

## WHEN/THEN Scenarios (from spec)

- WHEN a zone publish mutation arrives THEN it passes through override preemption, security gate, privacy decoration, attention gate, resource gate, and content resolution in order (spec lines 221-223)
- WHEN a frame contains 64 mutations (max batch) THEN each per-mutation policy check completes within 50us (total ~3.2ms) (spec lines 225-227)
- WHEN an agent attempts to create a tile without create_tiles capability THEN mutation rejected with CapabilityDenied error naming the missing capability (spec lines 131-133)
- WHEN an agent attempts to modify a tile in another agent's namespace THEN mutation rejected with NamespaceViolation (spec lines 135-136)
- WHEN a single capability check is performed THEN it completes in < 5us under nominal load (spec lines 139-141)
- WHEN runtime evaluates capability check THEN it uses snake_case canonical names (spec lines 286-288)
- WHEN capability grant uses read_scene, receive_input, or zone_publish:subtitle THEN startup fails with CONFIG_UNKNOWN_CAPABILITY with hint naming canonical replacement (spec lines 290-292)
- WHEN tile has private classification and viewer is known_guest THEN tile committed with redaction (spec lines 97-98)
- WHEN owner and guest both present THEN most restrictive viewer class (guest) applies (spec lines 100-102)
- WHEN sole viewer is owner THEN all content including sensitive shown without redaction (spec lines 104-106)
- WHEN agent declares public classification in zone with household default THEN effective classification is household (spec lines 113-115)
- WHEN mutation shed at Level 5 due to degradation THEN zone state updated but render output omitted (spec lines 173-175)
- WHEN degradation at Level 5 and CreateTile mutation arrives THEN mutation NOT shed, passes through (spec lines 177-179)
- WHEN two agents publish to LatestWins zone in same frame THEN second publish replaces the first (spec lines 186-188)
- WHEN agent publishes to tab_a/subtitle THEN it does not interact with tab_b/subtitle (spec lines 190-192)
- WHEN mutation shed at Level 5 THEN it has already passed Levels 3, 2, 4 (spec lines 360-362)
- WHEN 3 mutations rejected at Level 3 in single frame THEN PolicyTelemetry.mutations_rejected is 3 (spec lines 320-322)
- WHEN mutation rejected at Level 3 for capability denial THEN telemetry record emitted with event="arbitration_reject", level=3, code="CAPABILITY_DENIED" (spec lines 329-331)
- WHEN agent granted publish_zone:notification during handshake THEN structured log entry emitted (spec lines 339-340)
- WHEN Privacy (Level 2) redacts a tile THEN mutation committed (not rejected) but rendered with placeholder (Transform override type) (spec lines 247-249)
- WHEN Level 3 (Security) denies a capability THEN mutation rejected with structured error (Suppress override type) (spec lines 251-253)

## Test Scenes
- policy_matrix_basic: per-mutation evaluation with various capability/privacy combinations
- policy_arbitration_collision: full per-mutation pipeline under multi-level conflict

## Acceptance Criteria
- Per-mutation latency budget met (< 50us per mutation, < 3.2ms for batch of 64)
- Capability violations rejected with correct structured error (code + hint)
- Privacy redaction applied when viewer context requires it
- Zone ceiling rule enforced
- Level 5 shedding updates zone state without error to agent
- Transactional mutations never shed
- Content resolution uses correct ContentionPolicy
- Telemetry emitted for rejections, sheds, redactions
- Capability audit logging functional

## Purity Constraint
Policy evaluation MUST be a pure function over a typed PolicyContext — no side effects. The per-mutation pipeline reads capabilities, viewer context, budgets, and zone config as inputs; it does not write state.

## Crate/File Location
crates/tze_hud_policy/src/mutation.rs — per-mutation evaluation pipeline
crates/tze_hud_policy/src/security.rs — Level 3 capability checks
crates/tze_hud_policy/src/privacy.rs — Level 2 privacy evaluation, redaction
crates/tze_hud_policy/src/resource.rs — Level 5 resource enforcement
crates/tze_hud_policy/src/content.rs — Level 6 content resolution
crates/tze_hud_policy/src/telemetry.rs — PolicyTelemetry, ArbitrationTelemetryEvent

## v1-mandatory requirements
Per-Mutation Evaluation Pipeline, Level 3 Security Enforcement, Capability Registry Canonical Names, Level 2 Privacy Evaluation, Privacy Zone Ceiling Rule, Redaction Ownership, Level 5 Resource Enforcement, Level 6 Content Resolution, Degradation Does Not Bypass Arbitration, Policy Evaluation Latency Budgets (per-mutation), Policy Telemetry, Arbitration Telemetry Events, Capability Grant Audit

## v1-reserved exclusions
- Per-Agent Arbitration Diagnostics RPC (spec lines 376-383)

## post-v1 exclusions
- Dynamic Policy Rules (spec lines 367-374)

## What this does NOT include
- Arbitration stack data structures (bead #1)
- Per-event / per-frame pipelines (bead #3)
- Attention budget / quiet hours (bead #4)
- Compositor rendering of redaction placeholders (compositor epic)
- Shell state machine for freeze/safe-mode (Epic 11)
title: Per-mutation evaluation pipeline

description:
Implement lease priority and capability enforcement per lease-governance/spec.md Requirement: Priority Assignment (lines 49-60), Requirement: Priority Sort Semantics (lines 62-69), Requirement: Zone Publish Requires Active Lease (lines 213-224), Requirement: Lease Suspension Freezes Zone Publications (lines 226-233), Requirement: Lease Revocation Clears Zone Publications (lines 235-242), Requirement: Degradation Does Not Change Lease State (lines 262-269), Requirement: Tile Shedding Order (lines 271-278), and Requirement: Degradation Trigger Threshold (lines 280-288).

## Priority Assignment (spec lines 49-60, Requirement: Priority Assignment)
Every lease carries lease_priority: u8 field.
- Priority 0: reserved for system/chrome (runtime-internal only; agents MUST NOT request priority 0)
- Priority 1: requires lease:priority:1 capability (canonical name per RFC 0009 section 8.1, RFC 0006 section 6.3)
- Priority 2: default
- Agent requesting priority 0 receives priority 2
- Agent requesting priority 1 without capability receives priority 2
- Runtime MAY downgrade priority at any time via LeaseStateChange

Note: RFC 0008 section 2.1 uses the older name lease_priority_high; the canonical name is lease:priority:1.

## Priority Sort Semantics (spec lines 62-69, Requirement: Priority Sort Semantics)
Compositor sorts tiles by (lease_priority ASC, z_order DESC):
- Numerically lower lease_priority = higher priority (0 = highest)
- Within same priority class, higher z_order wins
- Tiles with highest lease_priority values and lowest z_order shed first

## Capability Scope
Per configuration/spec.md Requirement: Capability Vocabulary, canonical capability names include:
- create_tiles
- modify_own_tiles
- manage_tabs
- publish_zone:* (wildcard and per-zone-type: publish_zone:subtitle, etc.)
- read_scene_topology
- resident_mcp
- lease:priority:1

Mutations outside capability scope rejected with structured error.

## Zone Publish Requires Active Lease (spec lines 213-224, Requirement: Zone Publish Requires Active Lease)
For resident agents, zone publishing requires:
1. ACTIVE lease (not SUSPENDED, ORPHANED, or terminal)
2. publish_zone:<zone_type> capability for the target zone
3. Zone instance exists in current active tab
Error codes: LEASE_NOT_FOUND, LEASE_NOT_ACTIVE, CAPABILITY_NOT_GRANTED, ZONE_NOT_FOUND

## Lease Suspension Freezes Zone Publications (spec lines 226-233)
When suspended: existing publications visible but stale-badged; new publishes rejected with SAFE_MODE_ACTIVE; other agents continue independently.

## Lease Revocation Clears Zone Publications (spec lines 235-242)
When REVOKED or EXPIRED: all zone publications cleared from zone registry; zone contention policy governs replacement.

## Degradation Does Not Change Lease State (spec lines 262-269, Requirement: Degradation Does Not Change Lease State)
Degradation ladder (Levels 0-5) MUST NOT change lease states. Leases remain ACTIVE during all levels. At Level 4 (Shed Tiles), tiles removed from render pass but remain in scene graph with valid leases. At Level 5 (Emergency), only highest-priority tile plus chrome rendered; others visually suppressed but leases remain ACTIVE.

## Tile Shedding Order (spec lines 271-278, Requirement: Tile Shedding Order)
At Level 4: sort by (lease_priority ASC, z_order DESC), remove ~25% of active tiles per application. Shed tiles remain in scene graph; leases not revoked. On recovery, previously shed tiles resume rendering in priority order without agent re-publish.

## Degradation Trigger Threshold (spec lines 280-288, Requirement: Degradation Trigger Threshold)
Entry: frame_time_p95 > 14ms over 10-frame window.
Recovery: frame_time_p95 < 12ms over 30-frame window, one level at a time.

## WHEN/THEN Scenarios from Spec

Scenario: Priority 0 request downgraded (spec lines 54-56)
- WHEN an agent requests lease_priority = 0
- THEN the lease is granted with priority 2 (or the agent's capability ceiling)

Scenario: Priority 1 without capability (spec lines 58-60)
- WHEN an agent requests lease_priority = 1 without lease:priority:1 capability
- THEN the lease is granted with priority 2

Scenario: Shedding order (spec lines 67-69)
- WHEN the degradation ladder requires tile shedding
- THEN tiles with the highest lease_priority values (least important) and lowest z_order values are shed first

Scenario: Zone publish with suspended lease (spec lines 219-220)
- WHEN a resident agent attempts to publish to a zone while its lease is SUSPENDED
- THEN the publish is rejected with LEASE_NOT_ACTIVE

Scenario: Zone publish without capability (spec lines 222-224)
- WHEN an agent holds an ACTIVE lease but lacks publish_zone:subtitle and publishes to subtitle
- THEN the publish is rejected with CAPABILITY_NOT_GRANTED

Scenario: Stale zone content during suspension (spec lines 231-233)
- WHEN a lease is SUSPENDED
- THEN existing zone publications remain visible with a staleness indicator but no new publishes are accepted

Scenario: Zone cleared on revocation (spec lines 240-242)
- WHEN a lease is REVOKED
- THEN all zone publications from that lease are cleared and the zone's contention policy determines the replacement

Scenario: Lease active during tile shedding (spec lines 267-269)
- WHEN the degradation ladder reaches Level 4 and a tile is shed from the render pass
- THEN the tile's lease remains ACTIVE, the agent can still submit mutations, and the tile remains in the scene graph

Scenario: Shed tiles resume on recovery (spec lines 276-278)
- WHEN degradation recovers from Level 4 to Level 0
- THEN previously shed tiles resume rendering with their last committed content without any agent action

Scenario: Degradation entry (spec lines 285-287)
- WHEN frame_time_p95 > 14ms is sustained over a 10-frame window
- THEN the degradation ladder advances and tile shedding begins

## State Machine Diagram Reference
Priority and capability do not change the state machine transitions themselves. They affect grant-time decisions (priority clamping) and per-mutation authorization checks. Degradation does NOT change lease state (spec lines 262-269).

## Acceptance Criteria
- Priority-based shedding order matches spec sort key (lease_priority ASC, z_order DESC)
- Capability enforcement rejects unauthorized mutations with structured errors
- three_agents_contention test scene passes
- Priority 0 requests downgraded to priority 2
- Priority 1 without capability downgraded to priority 2
- Zone publish gating: LEASE_NOT_FOUND, LEASE_NOT_ACTIVE, CAPABILITY_NOT_GRANTED, ZONE_NOT_FOUND errors
- Degradation does not change lease state
- Tile shedding removes ~25% at Level 4, recovers on Level 0
- Degradation thresholds: entry at p95 > 14ms / 10 frames, recovery at p95 < 12ms / 30 frames

## Crate/File Location
crates/tze_hud_scene/src/lease/priority.rs (priority assignment, sort semantics, shedding order), crates/tze_hud_scene/src/lease/capability.rs (capability scope enforcement, zone publish gating), crates/tze_hud_scene/src/lease/degradation.rs (degradation ladder integration, tile shedding/recovery).

## Relevant Test Scenes
- three_agents_contention (validation-framework 25-scene registry)

## v1-mandatory Requirements Implemented
- Requirement: Priority Assignment (spec lines 49-60)
- Requirement: Priority Sort Semantics (spec lines 62-69)
- Requirement: Zone Publish Requires Active Lease (spec lines 213-224)
- Requirement: Lease Suspension Freezes Zone Publications (spec lines 226-233)
- Requirement: Lease Revocation Clears Zone Publications (spec lines 235-242)
- Requirement: Degradation Does Not Change Lease State (spec lines 262-269)
- Requirement: Tile Shedding Order (spec lines 271-278)
- Requirement: Degradation Trigger Threshold (spec lines 280-288)

## v1-reserved exclusions
- None specific to this bead

## post-v1 exclusions
- None specific to this bead

## What This Does NOT Include
- Core state machine transitions (bead #1)
- TTL countdown, renewal, or safe mode suspend/resume (bead #2)
- Disconnect grace or orphan handling (bead #3)
- Resource budget enforcement or three-tier ladder (bead #5)
- Compositor render pipeline changes (compositor epic responsibility)
title: Priority and capability scope — lease priority, shedding order, and capability enforcement

description:
BatchValidationError uses mutation_index = usize::MAX as a sentinel for batch-level errors (e.g. BatchSizeExceeded). This is awkward for cross-language consumers (serializes as 18446744073709551615) and relies on a magic value.

Fix: change mutation_index to Option<usize> where None = batch-level error, or add an explicit is_batch_level: bool field.

Discovered from rig-pkcv PR review (PR #127, thread PRRT_kwDORtExfM52UYHy).

close_reason: Resolved by RFC 0008 (PR #41) — lease suspension semantics defined, safe-mode contradiction resolved
description:
## Problem

RFC 0007 has a major internal contradiction between §4.2 (Dismiss All / Safe Mode) and §5.2 (Safe Mode Behavior):

- §4.2 says: 'All active leases are revoked simultaneously' when Dismiss All / Safe Mode is triggered.
- §5.2 says: 'Session suspension. All agent gRPC sessions receive SessionSuspended... Sessions are not terminated... all mutations are rejected with SAFE_MODE_ACTIVE until safe mode exits.'
- §5.5 says: On safe mode exit, 'Sessions transition from suspended to active. Agents receive SessionResumed... The compositor resumes applying pending scene mutations from the queue.'

These cannot both be true. If leases are revoked (§4.2), then on safe-mode exit there are no leases to resume, no sessions to unsuspend, and queued mutations reference revoked leases — they would all fail. The resume-with-queued-mutations model only makes sense if sessions are suspended (not terminated) and leases are suspended (not revoked).

## Affected Sections
- §4.2 (Dismiss All / Safe Mode)
- §5.2 (Safe Mode Behavior)
- §5.5 (Exit from Safe Mode)

## Relevant Doctrine
- failure.md: Core principle is 'the runtime must always be usable, even when agents are not.' The accidental safe-mode scenario (§5.2) strongly suggests suspend-not-revoke is the correct model.
- security.md §Human override: 'Entering a safe mode that disconnects all agents' — uses 'disconnects', not 'revokes leases', leaving room for either interpretation.

## Proposed Resolution
Change §4.2 to say leases are *suspended* (not revoked) when entering safe mode. Revocation language should only apply to individual tile dismiss (§4.1). Update §4.2 action list to:
1. All active leases are suspended simultaneously (not revoked).
2. All agent sessions receive SessionSuspended with reason viewer_safe_mode.
3. The runtime enters safe mode (§5).

This aligns with §5.2 and §5.5 and preserves the accidental-entry recovery path that the RFC explicitly motivates.
title: RFC 0007: Resolve safe-mode contradiction (revoke leases vs suspend-and-resume)

description:
# Local Feedback Guarantee

Implement the local-first feedback contract per input-model/spec.md. This is the doctrinal core:
"Local feedback first. Touch/interaction acknowledgement happens locally and instantly; remote semantics follow."

## Spec Requirements (with line numbers from spec.md)
- **Requirement: Local Feedback Latency — input_to_local_ack** (lines 152-154, spec.md) — Runtime updates local visual state (pressed, hovered, focused) at p99 < 4ms of input event arrival. Non-negotiable doctrinal rule. Local feedback path (Stages 1+2) executes entirely on main thread with no locks on mutable scene graph, using atomic snapshot of tile bounds. Combined Stage 1+2 budget < 1ms.
- **Requirement: Local Feedback Latency — input_to_scene_commit** (lines 163-165, spec.md) — Agent responses applied to scene graph within p99 < 50ms of input event arrival for local agents.
- **Requirement: Local Feedback Latency — input_to_next_present** (lines 174-176, spec.md) — Next frame containing local state updates presented within p99 < 33ms of input event arrival.
- **Requirement: Runtime-Owned Local State Updates** (lines 185-187, spec.md) — Runtime updates immediately without agent involvement: HitRegionLocalState.pressed on PointerDownEvent, HitRegionLocalState.hovered on PointerEnterEvent/PointerLeaveEvent, HitRegionLocalState.focused on focus transfer, tile scroll offset on ScrollEvent. Visual representations rendered by compositor, not by agent content.
- **Requirement: Local Feedback Rendering via SceneLocalPatch** (lines 196-198, spec.md) — Local state encoded in SceneLocalPatch, produced in Stage 2, containing LocalStateUpdate (node_id, pressed, hovered, focused) and ScrollOffsetUpdate (tile_id, offset_x, offset_y). SceneLocalPatch forwarded to compositor via dedicated channel separate from MutationBatch channel. Applied in Stage 4 before render encoding without lease validation or budget checks.
- **Requirement: Local Feedback Rollback on Agent Rejection** (lines 207-209, spec.md) — Agent explicitly rejects interaction: local feedback reverted with 100ms reverse animation. Rollback only on explicit rejection, NOT on agent latency or silence.
- **Requirement: Local Feedback Defaults and Customization** (lines 222-224, spec.md) — Default feedback: pressed=multiply by 0.85 (darkening), hovered=add 0.1 white overlay, focused=2px focus ring at node bounds. Defaults overridable per HitRegionNode via local_style field (LocalFeedbackStyle: hover_tint, press_tint, focus_ring_color, focus_ring_width_px).
- **Requirement: Scroll Local Feedback** (lines 233-235, spec.md) — Scroll is local-first. Compositor maintains scroll offset per scrollable tile, updates in same frame as scroll event without agent roundtrip. Tiles opt in via ScrollConfig. Scroll latency: input_to_local_ack p99 < 4ms. Agents receive ScrollOffsetChangedEvent (non-transactional, coalesced). User scroll overrides agent-set offset when both arrive in same frame.

## WHEN/THEN Acceptance Scenarios
- WHEN PointerDownEvent arrives at main thread THEN HitRegionLocalState.pressed set to true at p99 < 4ms and SceneLocalPatch produced and forwarded to compositor thread (line 158)
- WHEN PointerDownEvent arrives and local agent processes it and returns MutationBatch THEN mutation applied to scene graph within 50ms (p99) of original input event arrival (line 169)
- WHEN PointerDownEvent triggers pressed=true local state THEN next presented frame contains pressed visual state within 33ms (p99) of input event (line 180)
- WHEN pointer enters HitRegionNode's bounds THEN HitRegionLocalState.hovered set to true and hover visual (default: 0.1 white overlay) rendered by compositor in same frame without waiting for any agent response (line 191)
- WHEN pressed state change produced by Stage 2 THEN SceneLocalPatch applied by compositor without checking tile's lease status or budget (line 202)
- WHEN PointerDownEvent causes pressed=true and agent returns rejection THEN pressed set to false and 100ms reverse transition animation plays (line 213)
- WHEN PointerDownEvent causes pressed=true and agent does not respond within 50ms THEN pressed remains true until interaction ends naturally (line 217)
- WHEN HitRegionNode has local_style.press_tint set to Rgba(0.0, 0.0, 1.0, 0.3) and node is pressed THEN compositor applies custom blue tint instead of default 0.85 darkening (line 228)
- WHEN scroll wheel event arrives on tile with ScrollConfig(scrollable_y=true) THEN compositor updates tile's scroll offset_y in same frame without waiting for agent response (line 239)
- WHEN agent sends SetScrollOffsetRequest and user scroll event arrives in same frame THEN user scroll delta takes priority and agent request discarded (line 243)

## Latency budgets
- input_to_local_ack: < 4ms p99 (THE non-negotiable budget)
- Stage 1+2 combined: < 1ms p99
- input_to_scene_commit: < 50ms p99 (local agent response applied)
- input_to_next_present: < 33ms p99 (visual update in next frame)
- Scroll local feedback: < 4ms p99 (same as press state)
- Rollback animation: 100ms reverse transition

## Test scenes
- input_highlight — Layer 1 pixel tests for local feedback rendering
- chatty_dashboard_touch — scroll and hover under load
- Budget assertions: test_input_to_local_ack_p99_within_budget()

## Crate/file location
crates/tze_hud_input/src/ (local_feedback.rs, scene_local_patch.rs, scroll.rs)

## Depends on
- Sub-bead #2 (pointer event dispatch — produces events that trigger local feedback)
- Sub-bead #1 (focus tree — focus state is part of local feedback)
- Epic 2 (frame pipeline — compositor stages apply SceneLocalPatch)

## v1-mandatory
Local Feedback Latency (input_to_local_ack, input_to_scene_commit, input_to_next_present), Runtime-Owned Local State Updates, Local Feedback Rendering via SceneLocalPatch, Local Feedback Rollback on Agent Rejection, Local Feedback Defaults and Customization, Scroll Local Feedback

## v1-reserved exclusions
- Accessibility Tree V1 Fallback — a11y tree data structures shipped but platform API bridge deferred
- Gesture Recognition Latency — full gesture pipeline deferred; local feedback for gestures is v1-reserved

## post-v1 exclusions
- None specific to this bead

## Does NOT include
- Focus tree implementation (bead #1, though focus state updates are co-owned)
- Pointer event dispatch pipeline (bead #2 — produces events; this bead renders local feedback)
- Keyboard events / command input (bead #3, though ACTIVATE local feedback is co-owned)
- Event batching and coalescing (bead #4)
- Pointer capture protocol (bead #5)
title: Input: Local feedback guarantee

description:
Implement cross-agent sharing semantics for resources. Resources are global (not per-agent); any agent can reference any ResourceId if they know the hash — content-addressed identity is the capability. No ACL or ownership gate on reading. No enumeration operation exists.

## Parent epic
rig-tylk (Epic 10: Resource Store)

## Dependencies
- rig-etoq (RS-1: Content-addressed upload and deduplication)
- rig-qrri (RS-2: Reference counting and GC)

## Spec requirements (resource-store/spec.md)

### Requirement: Cross-Agent Resource Sharing (lines 166-168, v1-mandatory)
Resources are global, not per-agent. Any agent can reference any ResourceId if they know the hash. No ACL or ownership gate on reading. No "list all resources" enumeration operation — prevents resource discovery attacks.

### Requirement: Upload Capability Gate (lines 181-183, v1-mandatory)
Uploading requires upload_resource capability (included in default resident set). Guest agents cannot upload directly; they interact through MCP zone tools which handle resource management internally. Reading/referencing has no capability gate.

### Requirement: Per-Agent Budget Accounting for Shared Resources (lines 151-153, v1-mandatory)
Each referencing agent charged independently for the full decoded size. If Agent A and Agent B both reference the same resource, each charged the full decoded size (double-counting). Budget is per-agent, not per-resource.

### Requirement: Content-Addressed Resource Identity (lines 5-8, v1-mandatory)
ResourceId is a pure function of content bytes, independent of the uploading agent's namespace. This enables cross-agent sharing by hash alone.

## WHEN/THEN acceptance scenarios

WHEN Agent A uploads an image producing ResourceId X, and Agent B knows X
THEN Agent B MUST be able to create a node referencing ResourceId X without additional capability [line 172-173]

WHEN an agent attempts to list or enumerate all stored resources
THEN no such operation SHALL exist; agents can only query resources they know the ResourceId of [line 176-177]

WHEN a guest agent attempts to upload a resource directly
THEN the upload MUST be rejected with RESOURCE_CAPABILITY_DENIED [line 187-188]

WHEN Agent A (budget: 10 MiB) and Agent B (budget: 10 MiB) both reference a 4 MiB decoded texture
THEN Agent A MUST be charged 4 MiB and Agent B MUST be charged 4 MiB against their respective budgets [line 157-158]

WHEN Agent A and Agent B each create a node referencing the same ResourceId X, then Agent A deletes its node
THEN refcount MUST be 1 (Agent B's reference), and the resource MUST NOT be GC-eligible [line 142-143]

WHEN Agent A uploads a 500KB PNG and Agent B uploads the same 500KB PNG
THEN both MUST receive the same ResourceId (BLAKE3 hash of the raw bytes) [line 11-12]

## Relevant test scenes
Multi-agent sharing scenes, cross-namespace resource reference scenes from the validation-framework's 25-scene registry

## Crate/file location
crates/tze_hud_resource/ — sharing.rs (or integrated into store.rs access patterns)

## Wire vs debug format
ResourceId is raw 32 bytes on wire, hex for logging. Cross-agent sharing is implicit via content-addressed identity — no additional wire messages needed beyond standard resource reference in scene graph nodes.

## What this bead does NOT include
- Upload protocol (bead #1)
- Reference counting internals (bead #2) — this bead validates sharing semantics on top of refcount
- GC mechanics (bead #2)
- V1 ephemerality enforcement (bead #4)
- Resource enumeration API (explicitly excluded by spec — no such operation exists)
- ACL or ownership-based access control (explicitly excluded by spec)
- GPU texture compression BC7/ASTC (post-v1, spec lines 362-365)
- Persistent resource store (post-v1, spec lines 373-376)

## v1-mandatory
All requirements listed above.

## v1-reserved exclusions
None for this bead.

## post-v1 exclusions
- GPU Texture Compression BC7/ASTC (spec lines 362-365)
- Persistent Resource Store (spec lines 373-376)
- Post-V1 Resource Types (spec lines 384-386)
title: RS-3: Cross-agent resource sharing

description:
The batch pipeline Stage 1 lease check has two gaps:
1. batch.lease_id is added to lease_ids collection but not explicitly validated for existence/Active state — a nonexistent lease silently passes Stage 2 budget checks.
2. Non-CreateTile mutations (UpdateTileBounds, DeleteTile, SetTileRoot, AddNode) do not participate in the batch-level Stage 1 check; their lease validation is deferred to the graph layer checked methods.

Fix: (a) validate batch.lease_id explicitly in Stage 1 loop before budget check; (b) extend lease_id_for_mutation to derive lease from tile for tile-targeting mutations (requires graph lookup).

Discovered from rig-pkcv PR review (PR #127, threads PRRT_kwDORtExfM52UVUG, PRRT_kwDORtExfM52UYGs, PRRT_kwDORtExfM52UYIO).
