description:
Implement display profile selection, auto-detection, and budget enforcement per configuration/spec.md.

## Spec References
- Requirement: Display Profile full-display (spec.md lines 49-56, v1-mandatory)
- Requirement: Display Profile headless (spec.md lines 58-69, v1-mandatory)
- Requirement: Mobile Profile Schema-Reserved (spec.md lines 71-82, v1-mandatory)
- Requirement: Profile Auto-Detection (spec.md lines 84-99, v1-mandatory)
- Requirement: Profile Budget Escalation Prevention (spec.md lines 101-112, v1-mandatory)
- Requirement: Profile Extends Conflict Detection (spec.md lines 114-121, v1-mandatory)
- Requirement: Headless Virtual Display (spec.md lines 276-283, v1-mandatory)

## WHEN/THEN Acceptance Scenarios
- WHEN runtime starts with profile = "full-display" and no overrides THEN effective profile has max_tiles=1024, max_texture_mb=2048, max_agents=16, target_fps=60, min_fps=30 (lines 55-56)
- WHEN runtime starts with profile = "headless" THEN effective profile has max_tiles=256, max_texture_mb=512, max_agents=8, target_fps=60, min_fps=1 and renders to offscreen surface (lines 63-65)
- WHEN config sets [display_profile] extends = "headless" THEN startup fails with CONFIG_HEADLESS_NOT_EXTENDABLE (lines 68-69)
- WHEN config sets profile = "mobile" THEN startup fails with CONFIG_MOBILE_PROFILE_NOT_EXERCISED (not CONFIG_UNKNOWN_PROFILE) with hint to use full-display or headless (lines 77-78)
- WHEN config sets [display_profile].extends = "mobile" and [runtime].profile = "custom" THEN accepted with mobile budget values but no mobile runtime paths activated (lines 81-82)
- WHEN profile = "auto" and $DISPLAY/$WAYLAND_DISPLAY unset THEN selects headless and logs INFO naming detection signal (lines 90-91)
- WHEN profile = "auto" and GPU >4GB VRAM and refresh >= 60Hz THEN selects full-display (lines 94-95)
- WHEN profile = "auto" and display present but VRAM < 4GB THEN startup fails with structured error instructing explicit profile (lines 98-99)
- WHEN custom profile extends full-display and sets max_tiles = 2048 THEN startup fails with CONFIG_PROFILE_BUDGET_ESCALATION (lines 107-108)
- WHEN custom profile extends base with allow_background_zones = false and override sets true THEN fails with CONFIG_PROFILE_CAPABILITY_ESCALATION (lines 111-112)
- WHEN [runtime].profile = "full-display" and [display_profile].extends = "headless" THEN fails with CONFIG_PROFILE_EXTENDS_CONFLICTS_WITH_PROFILE (lines 120-121)
- WHEN profile = "headless" and headless_width = 1280 and headless_height = 720 THEN zone geometry computes against 1280x720 virtual surface (lines 282-283)

## Implementation Location
crates/tze_hud_config/src/profile.rs — profile resolution, auto-detection, budget validation

## Immutability Contract
Profile selection is frozen at startup. The resolved profile (full-display, headless, or custom) is immutable once validated. Profile changes require restart. Auto-detection runs once at startup only.

## v1-mandatory requirements
Display Profile full-display, Display Profile headless, Mobile Profile Schema-Reserved, Profile Auto-Detection, Profile Budget Escalation Prevention, Profile Extends Conflict Detection, Headless Virtual Display

## v1-reserved exclusions
- Mobile profile: profile = "mobile" is startup error; extends = "mobile" is valid but MPN display path not exercised
- Mobile never auto-selected by profile auto-detection

## post-v1 exclusions
- Mobile Presence Node (MPN) display path
- WebRTC media plane profile integration

## Does NOT include
- TOML parsing/loading (bead #1)
- Capability vocabulary (bead #3)
- Privacy/zones/agents (bead #4)

## Test Scenes
- Headless profile without display server
- Auto-detection in Docker/CI (no $DISPLAY)
- Custom profile budget escalation prevention
title: Config: Display profile resolution

close_reason: TelemetryFrame message defined in Round 3 with compositor performance fields; telemetry_frame field 41 in SessionMessage
description:
## Problem

§2.1 states: 'All scene mutations, event subscriptions, lease management, heartbeats, and **telemetry** are multiplexed over this single stream.' This echoes architecture.md §'Session model' which also mentions telemetry multiplexing.

However, the `SessionMessage` envelope oneof (§2.2 / §9) contains **no telemetry message type**. There is no `TelemetryEvent`, `TelemetryFrame`, or equivalent payload variant. The claim that telemetry is multiplexed over the session stream is unsubstantiated — the proto schema does not support it.

v1.md §'Telemetry' specifies: 'Per-frame structured telemetry (timing, throughput, resources, correctness)' and 'Per-session aggregates' as v1 deliverables. These need a delivery mechanism.

## Affected Sections

- §2.1 (Stream Topology — claims telemetry multiplexing)
- §2.2 (SessionMessage envelope — missing telemetry variant)
- §3.2 (Server→Client message table — no telemetry row)
- §9 (Proto schema — no telemetry message defined)

## Doctrine Reference

- architecture.md §'Session model': 'Scene mutations, event subscriptions, lease management, and telemetry are multiplexed over this single stream.'
- v1.md §'Telemetry': Per-frame structured telemetry is a v1 deliverable.
- architecture.md §'Node type extensibility': Each node type declares its telemetry surface.

## Concrete Change

**Option A (preferred):** Define a `TelemetryFrame` message and add it to the `SessionMessage` oneof. This message carries per-frame metrics (frame time, resource utilization, agent-specific stats). It should be state-stream class (reliable, coalesced). Add a `telemetry_frame` subscription category to §7.1.

```protobuf
message TelemetryFrame {
  uint64 frame_number = 1;
  uint64 frame_time_us = 2;          // Time to produce this frame
  uint64 agent_texture_bytes = 3;    // Agent's current texture memory usage
  uint32 active_lease_count = 4;     // Agent's active leases
  uint32 queued_mutations = 5;       // Mutations pending for this agent
  // Additional fields TBD by Telemetry RFC
}
```

**Option B:** Remove the telemetry claim from §2.1, acknowledge that telemetry delivery is out of scope for the session RFC, and defer to a dedicated Telemetry RFC. Update architecture.md to clarify that telemetry may use a separate delivery mechanism.

## Rationale

Claiming telemetry multiplexing without defining the message type creates a gap that implementers will fill inconsistently. Either define it or remove the claim.
title: RFC 0005: define telemetry message or remove telemetry multiplexing claim

description:
The vertical_slice example is the primary reference for how agents interact with the runtime. ChatGPT review and spec audit both identified it teaches legacy patterns that workers will copy.

**Current state (needs updating):**
- Requests legacy capability names (create_tile, create_node, receive_input) — should use canonical (create_tiles, modify_own_tiles, access_input_events)
- Prints 'Windowed mode not yet implemented; running headless demo' — needs windowed path once rig-f0k4 lands
- Does not demonstrate: registered-agent config flow, canonical subscription categories, zone publishing as primary LLM surface, structured error handling

1. Session init with canonical capability names
2. Capability negotiation (some granted, some denied)
3. Lease acquisition with priority
4. Zone publishing via publish_to_zone (the LLM-first surface)
5. Tile creation and mutation via resident capabilities
6. Subscription to SCENE_TOPOLOGY, LEASE_CHANGES (mandatory), ZONE_EVENTS
7. Structured error handling (capability denied, budget exceeded)
8. Graceful disconnect

- session-protocol/spec.md Requirement: MCP Bridge Guest Tools (lines 487-502)

description:
## Problem

RFC 0004 §1.3 defines focus cycling (Tab key traversal) order as:
> 'Traversal order follows tile z-order (lowest z first) and within each tile, tree order of HitRegionNode elements (depth-first, left-to-right sibling order).'

Z-ascending is a defensible default, but it is not universally the right keyboard-navigation order. Common cases where z-order diverges from desired tab order:
- A sidebar (high z for overlay purposes) that should be navigated last, not first
- A tile with high z-order for visual layering that is logically secondary in interaction flow
- Forms or wizard-style UIs where tab order follows reading order, not stacking order
- Accessibility best practices (WCAG 2.4.3) recommend tab order that matches visual/logical reading order, which may differ from z-order

Currently there is no way for an agent or configuration to override the default traversal order.

## Affected Sections
- §1.3 Focus Cycling (Tab Key Traversal) — the traversal order definition
- §5.7 Keyboard-Only Navigation — depends on the same traversal order
- §7.1 HitRegionNode — no tab-order field exists

## Concrete Change
1. Add an optional `tab_index: Option<i32>` field to `HitRegionNode` (§7.1) and to the corresponding protobuf `HitRegionNode` message (RFC 0001 §9). Semantics:
   - `None` / `0`: default (z-ascending, tree order) — current behavior
   - Positive integer: explicit tab order; lower values come first; elements with explicit tab_index come before elements with default order
   - Negative: excluded from tab cycle (equivalent to `tabindex=-1` in HTML)
2. Update §1.3 traversal algorithm: 'Elements with explicit tab_index are traversed first (ascending by tab_index), followed by elements with default ordering (z-ascending, tree order). Elements with negative tab_index are excluded from traversal but can still receive programmatic focus.'
3. Add tab_index to the a11y tree mapping (§5.3) for screen reader navigation consistency.

## Doctrine Reference
- presence.md §Interaction: 'keyboard-only navigation for all interactions' — effective keyboard navigation requires sensible tab order
- architecture.md §Error model: structured, machine-readable APIs — tab order should be explicit and inspectable
- WCAG 2.4.3 (Focus Order): 'focusable components receive focus in an order that preserves meaning and operability'

## Rationale
z-ascending is a good zero-configuration default, but real-world UIs frequently need custom tab order. Without this, agents building complex interactive surfaces will have to abuse z-order to achieve desired navigation patterns, conflicting with visual stacking needs.
title: RFC 0004: Add configurable tab-order metadata beyond z-ascending default

description:
Session protocol implementation epic — the bidirectional gRPC streaming protocol that is the single authoritative resident control path.

Implements the HudSession gRPC service with one bidirectional stream per agent. Covers session lifecycle, handshake/auth, mutation transport, lease management, subscription/event delivery, reconnection/resume, and MCP bridge.

## Primary spec
openspec/changes/v1-mvp-standards/specs/session-protocol/spec.md

## Secondary specs
- timing-model/spec.md (clock domains)
- lease-governance/spec.md (lease lifecycle)

## Dependencies (upstream)

- Epic 1: scene types (MutationProto, SceneSnapshot)
- Epic 3: clock sync (clock domain naming, estimated_skew_us)
- Epic 4: lease messages (LeaseRequest/LeaseResponse state machine)

## Depended on by (downstream)

## Canonical v1 message vocabulary
SessionInit, SessionEstablished, SessionError, SessionResume, SessionResumeResult, SessionClose, SessionSuspended, SessionResumed, LeaseRequest, LeaseResponse, LeaseStateChange, MutationBatch, MutationResult, SubscriptionChange, SubscriptionChangeResult, ZonePublish, ZonePublishResult, Heartbeat, EventBatch, InputFocusRequest, InputFocusResponse, InputCaptureRequest, InputCaptureResponse, InputCaptureRelease, SetImePosition, CapabilityRequest, CapabilityNotice, BackpressureSignal, DegradationNotice, RuntimeError, TelemetryFrame, SceneSnapshot, SceneDelta, ClientMessage, ServerMessage.

## Crate location

## v1-mandatory requirements covered

## v1-reserved exclusions

## post-v1 exclusions

title: Epic 6: Session Protocol

description:
The session-protocol spec already mandates removing the legacy unary SceneService (lines 26-37). But it doesn't specify the target proto file layout, and the current code has session.proto importing scene_service.proto to get shared types. When the unary service is removed, the shared types need a defined home.

**Current state (code):**
- scene_service.proto: mixed bag of shared types (Rect, Rgba, NodeProto, MutationProto, zone types, event types) AND the legacy SceneService definition AND legacy request/response wrappers
- session.proto: HudSession service + envelope messages, imports scene_service.proto for shared types

**Target proto layout (add as normative requirement):**
1. types.proto — Geometry primitives (Rect, Rgba), node types (NodeProto, SolidColorNodeProto, TextMarkdownNodeProto, HitRegionNodeProto, StaticImageNodeProto, ImageFitModeProto), mutation types (MutationProto, CreateTileMutation, SetTileRootMutation, PublishToZoneMutation, ClearZoneMutation), zone types (ZoneContent, ZoneDefinitionProto, ZonePublishRecordProto, ZoneRegistrySnapshotProto, GeometryPolicyProto, ContentionPolicyProto, RenderingPolicyProto, ZonePublishToken)
2. events.proto — InputEvent, InputEventKind, SceneEvent envelope, TileCreatedEvent, TileDeletedEvent, TileUpdatedEvent, LeaseEvent, LeaseEventKind
3. session.proto — HudSession service, ClientMessage/ServerMessage envelopes, session lifecycle (SessionInit, SessionResume, SessionClose, SessionAccepted, SessionDenied), MutationBatch/MutationResult, lease messages (LeaseRequest, LeaseRenew, LeaseRelease, LeaseGrant, LeaseDenial, LeaseStateChange), SubscriptionChange/Ack, Heartbeat, TelemetryFrame, SceneSnapshot, SceneDelta, BackpressureSignal

**Deleted (no new home):**
- SceneService (unary RPC service definition)
- ConnectRequest/ConnectResponse (replaced by SessionInit/SessionAccepted)
- LeaseRequest/LeaseResponse from unary API (replaced by session LeaseRequest/LeaseGrant)
- LeaseRenewRequest/Response, LeaseRevokeRequest/Response (replaced by LeaseRenew/LeaseRelease)
- MutationBatchRequest/Response (replaced by MutationBatch/MutationResult)
- EventSubscribeRequest (replaced by SubscriptionChange)
- SceneQueryRequest/Response (replaced by SceneSnapshot)
- ZoneRegistryRequest/Response (zone registry available via SceneSnapshot)

**Why this belongs in the spec, not just implementation:**
- Proto file layout is part of the wire protocol contract — it determines import paths and package namespaces
- Workers writing agents need stable import paths
- The split prevents accidental dependencies on legacy service-named files
- It aligns proto files with spec domains (scene-graph types, scene-events events, session-protocol session)
title: Add normative proto file layout to session-protocol spec

close_reason: PR #123 merged after PR review. Chrome layer sovereignty implementation reviewed and landed on main.
description:
# Chrome Layer Sovereignty

Implement the chrome layer rules that guarantee the system shell always renders on top of agent content and cannot be occluded, suppressed, or addressed by any agent.

## Ownership rule
Shell state transitions are the SOLE owner of chrome layer governance. No agent or policy subsystem can modify chrome rendering order, visibility, or hit-test priority.

## Spec references (system-shell/spec.md)

### Requirement: Chrome Layer Always On Top (line 10)
The chrome layer MUST render above all agent content in every frame. Compositor renders three ordered layers: background, content, chrome. Chrome shares the same wgpu pipeline as content (not a separate window/overlay/GPU context). No frame-level gap where agent content is visible without chrome.
- Scenario: Chrome above all agent tiles (line 15) — WHEN agent creates tile with max z-order THEN chrome renders on top
- Scenario: No frame without chrome (line 19) — WHEN compositor produces frame with agent tiles THEN chrome render pass executes in same frame after all content tiles composited

### Requirement: Agent Exclusion From Chrome (line 23)
Agents have no API to read, write, occlude, suppress, or receive events from chrome. Chrome elements do not appear in scene topology queries. Chrome elements not addressable via SceneId.
- Scenario: Scene topology excludes chrome (line 28) — WHEN agent queries scene topology THEN no chrome elements in result
- Scenario: No tile can occlude chrome (line 32) — WHEN agent requests tile z-order exceeding all others THEN tile renders below chrome

### Requirement: Chrome Rendering Independence (line 36)
Chrome rendering does not depend on any agent state. Chrome render pass reads exclusively from ChromeState, tab list, viewer context, cached layout geometry. If all agents crash, chrome renders correctly on next frame.
- Scenario: All agents crash (line 41) — WHEN all agent sessions terminate THEN chrome renders correctly on next frame

### Requirement: ChromeState Synchronization (line 45)
ChromeState protected by Arc<RwLock<ChromeState>> (or equivalent lock-free mechanism). Control plane holds write lock only for short-lived updates; compositor acquires read lock at start of chrome render pass, releases before GPU submit.
- Scenario: Concurrent read and write (line 50) — WHEN control plane updates badge state while compositor generates chrome render commands THEN no data races; compositor reads atomically

### Requirement: Tab Bar Rendering (line 54)
Tab bar displays tab names, active tab indicator, overflow badge. Position configurable (top, bottom, hidden). When hidden, keyboard shortcuts remain active. Tab bar does not display agent-supplied metadata.
- Scenario: Tab bar overflow (line 59) — WHEN more tabs than fit THEN +N indicator at trailing end; active tab scrolled into view
- Scenario: Hidden tab bar (line 63) — WHEN tab_bar_position = "hidden" THEN tab bar not rendered but Ctrl+Tab etc. still work

### Requirement: Tab Keyboard Shortcuts (line 67)
Ctrl+Tab (next), Ctrl+Shift+Tab (previous), Ctrl+1-8 (specific), Ctrl+9 (last). Handled before tile hit-testing. Never routed to agents.
- Scenario: Ctrl+Tab switches to next tab (line 72) — WHEN user presses Ctrl+Tab THEN active tab switches; no agent receives event

### Requirement: Dismiss Tile Override (line 76)
X button on hover/touch-hold. Activating: revokes lease, removes tile, frees resources, sends LeaseResponse with REVOKED and revoke_reason=VIEWER_DISMISSED. Works even if agent disconnected. Agent may re-request.
- Scenario: Dismiss an active tile (line 81) — WHEN viewer clicks X THEN tile removed within one frame, lease revoked, agent notified
- Scenario: Dismiss tile of disconnected agent (line 85) — WHEN viewer dismisses tile during reconnect grace period THEN grace period cancelled, tile removed, resources freed

### Requirement: Override Control Guarantees (line 206)
All overrides: local execution (no network roundtrip), frame-bounded response (visual within one frame <= 16.6ms), unconditional, no agent veto.
- Scenario: Override is frame-bounded (line 211) — WHEN viewer triggers dismiss THEN tile disappears within one frame
- Scenario: Override is unconditional (line 215) — WHEN viewer triggers safe mode while agent holds valid high-priority lease THEN safe mode activates regardless

### Requirement: Viewer Class Indicator (line 219)
Current viewer class displayed as icon in system status area. Icons: Owner (filled circle), Household (partial circle), Known guest (outline circle), Unknown (question mark), Nobody (dim circle). Cross-fade over 300ms on transitions.
- Scenario: Viewer class icon displayed (line 224) — WHEN viewer class is owner THEN filled circle icon appears
- Scenario: Viewer class transition animation (line 228) — WHEN class changes THEN icon cross-fades over 300ms

### Requirement: Agent Isolation for Viewer State (line 232)
No viewer state available to agents through any API. gRPC scene events exclude viewer class. MCP tools exclude viewer class. Redaction silent to agents. list_scene and scene topology responses omit viewer context.
- Scenario: Agent cannot detect viewer class (line 237) — WHEN agent queries scene topology THEN no viewer class information
- Scenario: Agent cannot detect redaction (line 241) — WHEN agent's tile is redacted THEN agent receives no notification

### Requirement: Shell Audit Events (line 245)
Every human override action and viewer-context change produces ShellAuditEvent to telemetry thread. Includes timestamp_mono_us, trigger, affected tile/session IDs. Never sent to agents.
- Scenario: Dismiss produces audit event (line 250) — WHEN viewer dismisses tile THEN ShellAuditEvent with tile_dismissed emitted
- Scenario: Audit events never reach agents (line 254) — WHEN any shell audit event emitted THEN routed to telemetry only

### Requirement: Audit Privacy Constraint (line 258)
Audit events must not contain viewer name, biometric features, auth details, device IDs, or geolocation. AuditViewerClassChanged carries only old_class and new_class.
- Scenario: Viewer class change audit (line 263) — WHEN viewer class changes THEN audit carries only old_class/new_class with no identity details

### Requirement: System Status Indicator (line 267)
Tab bar trailing end: health dot (green/amber/red), active agent count, viewer class icon, "Dismiss All" affordance. Does not expose agent identities.
- Scenario: Status indicator shows agent count (line 272) — WHEN 3 agents connected THEN green dot and "3 agents" with no names

### Requirement: Mute Control Surface (line 276, v1-reserved)
Chrome reserves mute control. In v1 rendered as disabled/greyed. Accepts input but noops. Ctrl+Shift+M shortcut.
- Scenario: Mute control noop (line 281) — WHEN viewer presses Ctrl+Shift+M THEN noop logged

### Requirement: V1 Diagnostic Surface (line 285)
Minimal diagnostic: scene graph dump, active leases, resource utilization, zone registry, telemetry snapshot. CLI only.
- Scenario: CLI scene graph dump (line 290) — WHEN operator requests dump THEN full scene state output

### Requirement: Capture-Safe Redaction Architecture (line 294)
Content and chrome render passes separable. V1 ships overlay-only redaction (capture_surface_active always false). Separation preserved for future render-skip.
- Scenario: Separable render passes (line 299) — WHEN compositor renders frame THEN content and chrome are separate passes

## Acceptance criteria
- Layer 1 pixel tests confirm chrome always visible above max-z-order agent tile
- Agent tile at max z-order still below chrome
- Chrome hit-test priority verified (chrome always wins before agent tiles)
- Scene topology queries return no chrome elements
- ChromeState concurrent access is race-free
- Tab bar renders with overflow indicator, hidden mode still has shortcuts
- Dismiss tile removes tile within 1 frame
- Viewer class icon renders correctly; transitions cross-fade over 300ms
- ShellAuditEvent emitted for all overrides; never routed to agents
- System status indicator shows health dot and agent count (no names)
- Mute control rendered disabled (v1-reserved)
- CLI diagnostic surface available
- Separable content/chrome render passes
- Test scenes: policy_matrix_basic (chrome visible during policy evaluation)

## v1-mandatory requirements
All requirements listed above are v1-mandatory except Mute Control Surface (v1-reserved) and Full TypeScript Inspector (post-v1).

## v1-reserved exclusions
- Mute Control Surface (line 276): rendered disabled, noop on input

## post-v1 exclusions
- Full TypeScript Inspector (line 303): GUI diagnostics deferred
- Render-skip redaction: capture_surface_active always false in v1
- Operator diagnostics overlay (GUI)

## Crate/file location
crates/tze_hud_runtime/src/shell/chrome.rs — chrome layer rendering, ChromeState struct, tab bar, viewer class indicator, system status indicator, audit events, diagnostic surface

## What this does NOT include
- Safe mode state machine (bead #2)
- Freeze semantics (bead #3)
- Privacy redaction logic (bead #4)
- Disconnection badges and backpressure signals (bead #5)
- Compositor layer stack implementation (Epic 2 prerequisite)
title: Shell #1: Chrome layer sovereignty

description:
## Problem

The ChatGPT 5.4 Pro external review flagged that `MutationBatch` carries an `agent_namespace` field (§3.1 Rust struct line: `pub agent_namespace: String`, and §7.1 protobuf: `string agent_namespace = 2`), even though the session protocol assigns the namespace during handshake authentication.

This creates a trust violation: the client tells the server who it is on every batch, rather than the server knowing who the client is from the authenticated session. This is a classic confused-deputy vulnerability — a malicious or buggy agent could set `agent_namespace` to another agent's namespace and attempt to mutate tiles it does not own.

## Why This Matters

- **Security:** security.md §'Capability scopes' says capabilities are granted per-session, not per-message. The namespace should be derived from the authenticated session, not from the message payload.
- **Redundancy:** The runtime already knows the agent's namespace from session auth. Carrying it in every batch is wasted bytes and a source of inconsistency.
- **Correctness:** The validation pipeline (§3.3) checks `T.namespace == batch.agent_namespace`, but this should be `T.namespace == session.namespace` — the server should never trust client-supplied identity.

## Sections Affected

- §3.1 `MutationBatch` Rust struct — remove `agent_namespace` field
- §3.3 Lease Check — change `batch.agent_namespace` to session-derived namespace
- §7.1 protobuf `MutationBatch` message — remove field 2 (`string agent_namespace`)
- §9.2 Transaction Pipeline diagram — remove namespace from batch label

## Doctrine Reference

security.md §'Authentication' — 'Every agent connection must be authenticated before any capability is granted.'
security.md §'Capability scopes' — 'Capabilities are granted per-session.'
presence.md §'Namespace Isolation' — 'Every agent session is assigned a namespace on authentication.'

## Concrete Changes Needed

1. Remove `agent_namespace` from `MutationBatch` Rust struct and protobuf message
2. Add a note that the namespace is derived from the authenticated session context, not from the batch payload
3. Update validation rules in §3.3 to reference session-derived namespace
4. Reserve protobuf field number 2 (mark as reserved to prevent reuse per proto3 conventions)
5. Update diagrams and text that reference `batch.agent_namespace`
title: RFC 0001: Remove client-supplied agent_namespace from MutationBatch wire contract

description:
## Problem

Capability names differ between the two RFCs that define them:

| Capability | RFC 0005 (Session) | RFC 0006 (Config) |
|---|---|---|
| Read scene topology | `read_scene` | `read_scene_topology` |
| Receive input events | `receive_input` | `access_input_events` |
| Zone publishing | `zone_publish:<zone>` | `publish_zone:<zone>` |

These are the same capabilities with different names — a permanent compatibility debt if both RFCs are treated as normative. Agents configured via RFC 0006 TOML will use one vocabulary; the session handshake (RFC 0005) will use another. Either the config parser or the session layer must translate, adding unnecessary complexity and bug surface.

Per-RFC bead rig-anb exists for the RFC 0005 side of this fix.

## Concrete Work

1. **Choose canonical names**: Recommend RFC 0006 names as canonical (more descriptive):
   - `read_scene_topology` (not `read_scene`)
   - `access_input_events` (not `receive_input`)
   - `publish_zone:<zone_type>` (not `zone_publish:<zone>`)
2. **Update RFC 0005** §3/§4: replace all capability references with canonical names. Update CapabilityGrant proto enum/string values.
3. **Update RFC 0006** §5: verify names match (should already, since we're adopting its vocabulary)
4. **Define capability registry**: Create a dedicated section (in RFC 0005, RFC 0006, or the proposed Policy RFC 0009) listing ALL capabilities with:
   - Canonical name
   - Description
   - Scope (per-session, per-zone, global)
   - Default grant policy (granted by default vs requires explicit config)
5. **Audit all 7 RFCs** for any other capability references that use non-canonical names

## Sections Affected

- RFC 0005 §3 (SessionInit capability fields)
- RFC 0005 §4 (CapabilityGrant proto)
- RFC 0006 §5 (capability configuration)
- Potentially RFC 0009 (Policy RFC) as the long-term authority

## Related Beads

- rig-anb: RFC 0005 side of capability vocabulary unification
- rig-em4: Policy & Arbitration RFC (capability scopes are a policy concern)

## Acceptance Criteria

1. Single canonical capability vocabulary used in all RFCs
2. Capability registry with name, description, scope, default policy
3. No remaining name mismatches between session and config
4. Proto schema CapabilityGrant uses canonical names
title: Cross-RFC pass: unify capability vocabulary between RFC 0005 and RFC 0006

Secondary specs: validation-framework/spec.md (Layer 1, Layer 3)

description:
Sub-bead #1 of Epic 3 (Timing Model). Implement clock domain naming convention and type-safe timestamp wrappers.

Parent epic: rig-zyeo (Epic 3: Timing Model)
Dependencies: Epic 0 (rig-ge28, test infrastructure), Epic 1 (scene types for timestamp fields — not yet created)

## Spec requirements (v1-mandatory)

### Requirement: Clock Domain Naming Convention (spec lines 23-30)
All timestamp fields MUST encode their clock domain in the field name suffix: _wall_us for UTC wall clock (network clock domain) and _mono_us for monotonic system clock. Fields MUST NOT mix wall and mono in the same field. Fields without a clock-domain suffix are deltas or frame-relative values, not timestamps.

WHEN/THEN scenarios:
- WHEN a new timestamp field is added to any proto or Rust struct, THEN it MUST use _wall_us for UTC wall clock or _mono_us for monotonic clock; generic _us suffix without domain indicator MUST NOT be used for timestamps (spec line 29-30)

### Requirement: Clock Domain Separation (spec lines 10-21)
tze_hud MUST recognize four distinct clock domains: Display clock (vsync-driven), Monotonic system clock (OS monotonic, microsecond resolution), Network clock (agent-supplied UTC microseconds), and Media clock (GStreamer pipeline, post-v1 only). These domains MUST NOT be interchangeable.

WHEN/THEN scenarios:
- WHEN the compositor makes presentation timing decisions, THEN the display clock (vsync signal) MUST be the master reference (spec lines 15-17)
- WHEN the compositor measures stage latencies, deadline calculations, or lease TTL, THEN it MUST use the monotonic system clock (spec lines 19-21)

### Requirement: Timestamp Resolution and Format (spec lines 63-70)
All timestamps in the public API MUST use UTC microseconds since Unix epoch as uint64. Zero (0) MUST always mean "not set."

WHEN/THEN scenarios:
- WHEN a timestamp field contains value 0, THEN the runtime MUST interpret it as "not set" (spec lines 68-70)

### Requirement: Injectable Clock (spec lines 313-324)
All timing paths MUST use an injectable clock source (Clock trait with now_us() and monotonic_us() methods). SystemClock (production) and SimulatedClock (tests, manually advanced) MUST be provided. The Clock trait MUST NOT have advance or set_time methods.

WHEN/THEN scenarios:
- WHEN a test uses SimulatedClock and advances time by 100ms, THEN all timing-dependent behavior MUST respond to the simulated time (spec lines 318-320)
- WHEN the Clock trait is used in production code, THEN it MUST only provide now_us() and monotonic_us(); no mutation methods (spec lines 322-324)

## Implementation

Recommended approach (goes beyond what spec literally requires but prevents the class of bugs the convention targets):
- WallUs newtype wrapper over u64 — enforces _wall_us domain at compile time
- MonoUs newtype wrapper over u64 — enforces _mono_us domain at compile time
- DurationUs for deltas (not a timestamp, no clock domain)
- Conversion between WallUs and MonoUs is explicit and requires a calibration offset
- Passing WallUs where MonoUs is expected MUST fail to compile

Existing code: crates/tze_hud_scene/src/clock.rs already has Clock trait, SystemClock, and TestClock. This bead extends that with domain types and validates the naming convention.

## Crate/file location
- Primary: crates/tze_hud_scene/src/clock.rs (extend existing Clock trait and types)
- New module: crates/tze_hud_scene/src/timing/domains.rs (WallUs, MonoUs, DurationUs newtypes)

## Relevant test scenes
- All 25 scenes use timestamps — this is foundational type infrastructure

## Cross-epic contracts
- Session-protocol (Epic 6) uses WallUs/MonoUs for session_open_wall_us, session_open_mono_us
- Input-model (Epic 5) uses MonoUs for interaction timestamps
- All scene types (Epic 1) must adopt _wall_us/_mono_us suffixes

## Acceptance criteria
- All timestamp fields in scene/protocol/session crates use correct _wall_us/_mono_us suffixes
- WallUs/MonoUs newtypes prevent cross-domain assignment at compile time
- Zero-value semantics (0 = "not set") encoded in type API
- Clock trait remains observation-only (no mutation methods in trait)
- SimulatedClock enables deterministic test timing

## v1-mandatory
- Clock Domain Separation, Clock Domain Naming Convention, Timestamp Resolution and Format, Injectable Clock

## v1-reserved exclusions
- None

## post-v1 exclusions
- Media clock domain (GStreamer pipeline clock, nanosecond resolution) — defined but not implemented
- ClockedMediaCue message class — proto enum value only

## Scope boundary (what this bead does NOT include)
- TimingHints struct (sub-bead #2)
- Presentation scheduling logic (sub-bead #2)
- Sync groups (sub-bead #3)
- Relative scheduling (sub-bead #4)
title: Clock domain types and validation

description:
# Pointer Capture Protocol

Implement pointer capture per input-model/spec.md.

## Spec Requirements (with line numbers from spec.md)
- **Requirement: Pointer Capture Semantics** (lines 104-106, spec.md) — Only one node holds pointer capture at a time globally across entire scene. Capture associated with specific pointer device (device_id). Capture only acquired in response to PointerDownEvent (not PointerMove or PointerUp). While captured, all pointer events from captured device routed to capturing node, bypassing normal hit-testing.
- **Requirement: Capture Release** (lines 119-121, spec.md) — Released on: explicit CaptureReleaseRequest from owning agent, PointerUpEvent for captured device (when release_on_up=true), or runtime capture theft. CaptureReleasedEvent dispatched with reason: AGENT_RELEASED, POINTER_UP, RUNTIME_REVOKED, LEASE_REVOKED.
- **Requirement: Capture Theft by Runtime** (lines 130-132, spec.md) — Runtime revokes capture unconditionally for: Alt+Tab (window switch), system notifications requiring full screen, agent lease revocation, user-initiated tab switch. On theft: PointerCancelEvent sent first, then CaptureReleasedEvent(reason=RUNTIME_REVOKED). Agent MUST treat PointerCancelEvent as terminal.
- **Requirement: Auto-Capture on PointerDown** (lines 141-143, spec.md) — HitRegionNode with auto_capture=true: runtime automatically acquires pointer capture for that node on PointerDownEvent without explicit CaptureRequest from agent.

## WHEN/THEN Acceptance Scenarios
- WHEN node N1 in tile T1 holds pointer capture for device D1 and pointer moves outside T1's bounds THEN all pointer events from D1 routed to N1 regardless of which tile pointer is visually over (line 110)
- WHEN node N1 holds capture for device D1 and node N2 requests capture for same D1 THEN runtime responds with CaptureResponse(result=DENIED) (line 114)
- WHEN node with release_on_up=true holds capture and PointerUpEvent arrives for captured device THEN capture released and CaptureReleasedEvent(reason=POINTER_UP) dispatched to owning agent (line 125)
- WHEN node holds pointer capture and user presses Alt+Tab THEN runtime sends PointerCancelEvent to capturing node, followed by CaptureReleasedEvent(reason=RUNTIME_REVOKED) (line 136)
- WHEN PointerDownEvent hits HitRegionNode with auto_capture=true THEN runtime acquires capture for that node and device without agent sending CaptureRequest (line 147)

## Latency budgets
- Capture routing: no additional latency beyond normal dispatch (< 4ms p99 input_to_local_ack)
- Capture theft: PointerCancelEvent + CaptureReleasedEvent delivered within same frame as system event
- Hit-test bypass: captured events skip hit-test entirely (< 100µs savings)

## Test scenes
- input_highlight — capture routes events correctly
- chatty_dashboard_touch — auto-capture for press-drag-release

## Crate/file location
crates/tze_hud_input/src/ (capture.rs, pointer_capture.rs)

## Depends on
- Sub-bead #1 (focus tree — capture interacts with focus on theft/revocation)
- Sub-bead #2 (pointer event dispatch — capture redirects pointer event routing)

## v1-mandatory
Pointer Capture Semantics, Capture Release, Capture Theft by Runtime, Auto-Capture on PointerDown

## v1-reserved exclusions
- None specific to pointer capture (all capture requirements are v1-mandatory)

## post-v1 exclusions
- Drag-and-Drop Protocol — cross-tile DnD is post-v1; v1 agents implement custom protocol over pointer events + capture

## Does NOT include
- Focus tree (bead #1)
- Pointer event dispatch pipeline (bead #2 — produces events; this bead redirects routing)
- Keyboard events / command input (bead #3)
- Event batching and coalescing (bead #4)
- Local feedback rendering (bead #6)
title: Input: Pointer capture protocol

description:
# Epic 8: Policy Arbitration

The policy arbitration system — the fixed 7-level precedence stack that resolves conflicts between human overrides, safety, privacy, security, attention, resources, and content. Policy evaluation is a pure function over a typed PolicyContext — no side effects. State transitions (freeze, safe mode) belong to system-shell.

## Dependencies
- Epic 0 (rig-ge28: PolicyEvaluator trait contract, test infrastructure)
- Epic 4 (lease governance — lease priority, not yet created as bead)
- Epic 7 (rig-nolr: capabilities, quiet hours, configuration)

## Depended on by
- Epic 9 (events filtered by policy)
- Epic 11 (shell invokes policy for overrides)

## Primary spec
openspec/changes/v1-mvp-standards/specs/policy-arbitration/spec.md

## Secondary specs
- lease-governance/spec.md (priority)
- configuration/spec.md (capabilities, quiet hours)

## Sub-beads (4)
1. Seven-level arbitration stack
2. Per-mutation evaluation pipeline
3. Per-event and per-frame evaluation
4. Attention budget and quiet hours

## v1-mandatory requirements covered
Seven-Level Arbitration Stack (lines 10-17), Cross-Level Conflict Resolution (lines 19-46), Level 0 Human Override (lines 48-59), Level 1 Safety Response (lines 61-72), GPU Failure Three-Tier Response (lines 74-89), Level 2 Privacy Evaluation (lines 91-106), Privacy Zone Ceiling Rule (lines 108-115), Redaction Ownership (lines 117-124), Level 3 Security Enforcement (lines 127-141), Level 4 Attention Management (lines 143-166), Level 5 Resource Enforcement (lines 168-179), Level 6 Content Resolution (lines 181-192), Per-Frame Evaluation Pipeline (lines 194-205), Per-Event Evaluation Pipeline (lines 207-214), Per-Mutation Evaluation Pipeline (lines 216-227), ArbitrationOutcome Types (lines 229-241), Override Type Taxonomy (lines 242-253), Override Composition (lines 255-267), Freeze Semantics at Level 0 (lines 268-279), Capability Registry Canonical Names (lines 281-292), Policy Evaluation Latency Budgets (lines 294-312), Policy Telemetry (lines 315-322), Arbitration Telemetry Events (lines 324-331), Capability Grant Audit (lines 333-340), Within-Level Conflict Resolution (lines 342-354), Degradation Does Not Bypass Arbitration (lines 355-362)

## v1-reserved exclusions
- Per-Agent Arbitration Diagnostics RPC (spec lines 376-383) — v1 diagnostics via telemetry only

## post-v1 exclusions
- Dynamic Policy Rules (spec lines 367-374) — all v1 policy rules are static, derived from configuration at load time
- Freeze/safe-mode state transitions — owned exclusively by system-shell (spec lines 364-366)

## Crate location
crates/tze_hud_policy/src/ (new crate; pure functions, no side effects)

## Test scenes
- policy_matrix_basic (validation-framework 25-scene registry)
- policy_arbitration_collision (validation-framework 25-scene registry)

## Architecture invariant
Policy evaluation MUST be a pure function over a typed PolicyContext — no side effects. State transitions belong to shell/lease modules. Cross-reference: "Freeze and Safe Mode State Transitions" (spec lines 364-366).

description:
Implement the binary identity contract for SceneId and ResourceId per scene-graph/spec.md.

## Spec References

### Requirement: SceneId Identity (scene-graph/spec.md line 10)
SceneId: 16-byte little-endian UUIDv7, monotonically increasing, unique within runtime lifetime. All-zero SceneId ([0u8; 16]) means null/absent (no root node, no sync group membership). SceneId provides lexicographic sortability by creation time for sequence ordering and log correlation.
Source: RFC 0001 §1.1, §4.1 | Scope: v1-mandatory

### Requirement: ResourceId Identity (scene-graph/spec.md line 23)
ResourceId: 32-byte raw BLAKE3 content hash (NOT hex-encoded), equality by byte comparison. Two agents uploading identical content MUST receive the same ResourceId; the runtime SHALL store the resource once. Hex encoding is a display/debug concern only and MUST NOT appear on the wire or in storage.
Source: RFC 0001 §1.1 | Scope: v1-mandatory

### Requirement: Zone Publishing (scene-graph/spec.md line 198, partial)
Zone publication requires both publish_zone:<zone_name> capability AND a valid ZonePublishToken. Referenced here because ZonePublishToken is an identity type that must be defined alongside SceneId/ResourceId.

## WHEN/THEN Scenarios from Spec

- Scenario: SceneId generation (line 15)
  WHEN a new scene object (tab, tile, node) is created
  THEN the runtime MUST assign it a UUIDv7 SceneId that is unique within the runtime instance and lexicographically sortable by creation time

- Scenario: SceneId zero value (line 19)
  WHEN a SceneId field contains all-zero bytes ([0u8; 16])
  THEN the runtime MUST interpret it as "absent/null"

- Scenario: Content deduplication (line 28)
  WHEN two agents upload an identical PNG image
  THEN both MUST receive the same ResourceId and the runtime MUST store only one copy

- Scenario: ResourceId is 32 bytes (resource-store/spec.md line 14)
  WHEN a ResourceId is computed for any resource
  THEN it MUST be exactly 32 bytes (BLAKE3 output)

## Implementation Details

Both types must implement: Eq, Hash, Ord, Clone, Copy, serialization to/from protobuf bytes.
SceneId must use UUIDv7 (uuid crate with v7 feature). Generation must be monotonic within a runtime instance.
ResourceId must use blake3 crate for hashing.
Both must serialize cleanly to/from protobuf bytes fields in types.proto.

## Acceptance Criteria

- All Layer 0 identity invariant checks from Epic 0 pass
- SceneId generation is monotonic (property test: generate 10,000 IDs, assert sorted)
- All-zero SceneId is treated as null/absent (is_null() method returns true)
- ResourceId deduplication works by byte equality (same content → same ResourceId)
- ResourceId is exactly 32 bytes, never hex-encoded in wire/storage representation
- size_of::<SceneId>() == 16, size_of::<ResourceId>() == 32
- Protobuf round-trip: SceneId/ResourceId → bytes → SceneId/ResourceId lossless

## Epic 0 Test Gates

- Layer 0 identity invariant checks in assert_layer0_invariants()
- Property tests from Epic 0 proptest suite covering SceneId uniqueness and monotonicity
- Test scenes: all 25 scenes use SceneId for object identity — this bead provides the foundation type

## Crate/File Location

- Primary: crates/tze_hud_scene/src/types.rs (SceneId, ResourceId already partially defined)
- Protobuf mapping: crates/tze_hud_protocol/ (types.proto bytes field conversion)
- Tests: crates/tze_hud_scene/src/types.rs (unit tests) and crates/tze_hud_scene/tests/ (integration)

## v1 Scope Tags

- v1-mandatory: SceneId Identity, ResourceId Identity (scene-graph/spec.md lines 10-30)
- v1-reserved: None for this bead
- post-v1: Persistent resource store (resource-store/spec.md) — ResourceId defined now, persistence deferred

## What This Does NOT Include

- Scene hierarchy (bead #2)
- Zone publishing logic (bead #4) — only the ZonePublishToken identity type is defined here
- Resource store implementation (separate epic) — only the ResourceId type
- Protobuf service definitions — only type serialization helpers
title: SceneId and ResourceId identity types

description:
In session_server.rs handle_mutation_batch and handle_zone_publish, a fresh batch_id is generated rather than mapping from the proto MutationBatch. This breaks rejection correlation semantics (BatchRejected.batch_id won't match the client's batch_id).

Additionally lease_id: None is set, bypassing batch-level lease validation for non-CreateTile mutations in the gRPC path.

Fix: map batch.batch_id from the proto field; set lease_id from the authenticated session's lease_id field.

Scope: session-protocol epic. Discovered from rig-pkcv PR review (PR #127, thread PRRT_kwDORtExfM52UYJL).

description:
Sub-bead #4 of Epic 3 (Timing Model). Implement relative scheduling fields (after_us, frames_from_now, next_frame) as wire-level convenience sugar over absolute present_at_wall_us.

Parent epic: rig-zyeo (Epic 3: Timing Model)
Depends on: sub-bead #2 (rig-mdpo, TimingHints and presentation scheduling)

## Spec requirements (v1-mandatory)

### Requirement: Relative Scheduling Primitives (spec lines 270-289)
The compositor MUST support three relative scheduling fields as wire-level convenience sugar:
- after_us: N microseconds from compositor monotonic clock at Stage 3 intake
- frames_from_now: N display frames from current frame
- next_frame: sugar for frames_from_now = 1

These MUST be converted to absolute present_at_wall_us at Stage 3 intake and MUST never be stored in the scene graph, telemetry, or internal state.

WHEN/THEN scenarios:
- WHEN an agent submits a mutation with after_us = 500,000 (500ms), THEN the compositor MUST compute target_mono_us = monotonic_us_at_intake + 500,000, convert to wall-clock via session clock-skew estimate (present_at_wall_us = target_mono_us + skew_us), and enter the mutation into the pending queue at Stage 3 intake (spec lines 275-277)
- WHEN an agent submits a mutation with frames_from_now = 3 at frame N, THEN the compositor MUST convert it to present_at_wall_us corresponding to frame N+3 vsync and apply at that frame (spec lines 279-281)
- WHEN an agent submits next_frame = true, THEN it MUST be treated identically to frames_from_now = 1; the mutation MUST NOT be applied in the current frame (spec lines 283-285)
- WHEN a relative scheduling field is converted to present_at_wall_us, THEN the raw relative value MUST NOT appear in any scene graph state, telemetry record, or stored mutation (spec lines 287-289)

### Interaction with Timing Fields on Payloads (spec lines 72-83)
The oneof schedule (present_at_wall_us, after_us, frames_from_now, next_frame) MUST be mutually exclusive; setting more than one MUST be rejected with RELATIVE_SCHEDULE_CONFLICT.

WHEN/THEN scenarios:
- WHEN an agent sets both present_at_wall_us and after_us in the same message, THEN the compositor MUST reject the batch with RELATIVE_SCHEDULE_CONFLICT (spec lines 81-83)

## Implementation notes
- Relative scheduling is "sugar over absolute" — the prompt's description of after(duration) and with(tile_id) maps to the spec's after_us and frames_from_now/next_frame
- The spec does not include a with(tile_id) primitive; the prompt's "with(tile_id): present simultaneously with content in another tile" is not in the spec. This is noted as a potential conflict per PREAMBLE.md authority rules. The spec's relative primitives are after_us, frames_from_now, and next_frame only.
- Conversion happens at Stage 3 intake only — after conversion, the mutation enters the same pending queue as absolute-scheduled mutations
- Requires access to: monotonic clock at intake time, session clock-skew estimate, current frame number and vsync timing

## Conflict note (per PREAMBLE.md)
The prompt (03-timing-model.md) describes:
  - after(duration): present N microseconds after the previous content in the same tile
  - with(tile_id): present simultaneously with content in another tile
The spec (timing-model/spec.md) defines:
  - after_us: N microseconds from compositor monotonic clock at Stage 3 intake (NOT "after previous content")
  - frames_from_now: N display frames from current frame
  - next_frame: sugar for frames_from_now = 1
  - No with(tile_id) primitive exists in the spec
Per PREAMBLE.md: "Specs are authoritative." This bead follows the spec definition, not the prompt paraphrase.

## Crate/file location
- crates/tze_hud_scene/src/timing/relative.rs (new: conversion functions for after_us, frames_from_now, next_frame)
- Integration with Stage 3 intake in crates/tze_hud_scene/src/timing/scheduling.rs (from sub-bead #2)

## Relevant test scenes
- All scenes that submit mutations — relative scheduling is a convenience path into the same absolute pipeline
- coalesced_dashboard — high-frequency state-stream mutations may use after_us
- chatty_dashboard_touch — ephemeral mutations may use next_frame

## Cross-epic contracts
- Session-protocol (Epic 6) provides session clock-skew estimate needed for after_us -> wall-clock conversion
- Scene types (Epic 1) carry relative scheduling fields on MutationBatch proto as oneof schedule

## Acceptance criteria
- after_us correctly converted to absolute present_at_wall_us using monotonic clock + skew estimate
- frames_from_now correctly mapped to target frame's vsync_wall_us
- next_frame treated identically to frames_from_now = 1
- Converted mutations enter same pending queue as absolute-scheduled mutations
- Raw relative values NEVER appear in scene graph state, telemetry, or stored mutations
- Mutual exclusivity enforced: setting multiple schedule fields produces RELATIVE_SCHEDULE_CONFLICT
- Composition with absolute timing verified (relative is sugar, not a separate path)
- All tested with SimulatedClock for deterministic behavior

## v1-mandatory
- Relative Scheduling Primitives (spec lines 270-289)

## v1-reserved exclusions
- None

## post-v1 exclusions
- Media Clock Integration (spec lines 404-411) — no media-clock-relative scheduling
- with(tile_id) primitive — not in spec; if needed, would be a post-v1 addition

## Scope boundary (what this bead does NOT include)
- Clock domain types (sub-bead #1)
- TimingHints struct, presentation scheduling, pending queue, expiration (sub-bead #2)
- Sync groups (sub-bead #3)
- Absolute presentation scheduling engine (sub-bead #2 — this bead only converts relative to absolute)
title: Relative scheduling primitives

description:
Proto files still carry legacy wire formats alongside modern ones:

**types.proto:**
- NodeProto.id is 'string' (line 39) — should be 'bytes' (16-byte UUIDv7 SceneId)
- CreateTileMutation.tab_id is 'string' (line 98) — should be 'bytes'
- SetTileRootMutation.tile_id is 'string' — should be 'bytes'

**events.proto:**
- InputEvent.timestamp_ms (line 19) — should be timestamp_mono_us
- TileCreatedEvent.timestamp_ms (line 38) — should be timestamp_wall_us
- TileDeletedEvent.timestamp_ms (line 43) — should be timestamp_wall_us
- TileUpdatedEvent.timestamp_ms (line 48) — should be timestamp_wall_us
- LeaseEvent.timestamp_ms (line 58) — should be timestamp_wall_us
- SceneEvent.timestamp_ms (line 74) — should be timestamp_wall_us

Note: newer RFC 0004 events in events.proto already use correct timestamp_mono_us and bytes IDs. The legacy family above coexists in the same file.

**What must change:**
1. Migrate all string ID fields to bytes (SceneIdProto) in types.proto
2. Migrate all timestamp_ms fields to correct clock-domain suffix (_wall_us or _mono_us) in events.proto
3. Update all Rust code that reads/writes these fields (session_server.rs, convert.rs, vertical_slice)
4. Remove or deprecate the legacy InputEvent/SceneEvent family if fully superseded by RFC 0004 events

**Spec refs:**
- scene-graph/spec.md Requirement: SceneId Identity (lines 10-21) — 16-byte UUIDv7, bytes
- timing-model/spec.md Requirement: Clock Domain Naming Convention (lines 23-30) — _wall_us/_mono_us
- session-protocol/spec.md Requirement: Proto File Layout (lines 41-99)

**v1-mandatory. Files: proto/types.proto, proto/events.proto, session_server.rs, convert.rs**

description:
The new proto layout requirement (lines 41-99) correctly defines the three-file layout (types.proto/events.proto/session.proto) and mandates scene_service.proto deletion. But three later sections still reference the old layout:

**Line 611 (Zone Publishing requirement):**
'ZoneContent from scene_service.proto' — should say 'ZoneContent from types.proto' (it's correctly listed at line 48)

**Line 648 (Protobuf Schema requirement):**
'It SHALL import scene_service.proto for MutationProto, SceneEvent, InputEvent, LeaseRequest, LeaseResponse, SceneSnapshot, and zone types. It SHALL import scene.proto for SceneId.'
— This entire requirement contradicts lines 41-99. Should say session.proto imports types.proto and events.proto. The listed types should reference their correct homes per lines 44-56. Also defines ClockSync RPC which isn't in the three-file layout.

**Line 654 (Single gRPC service scenario):**
'it SHALL use the SessionService with the Session RPC' — should say 'HudSession' per lines 27/33/37/59.

**Fix:** Rewrite lines 611, 647-654 to be consistent with the three-file layout defined at lines 41-99. This is a straightforward text alignment — the correct information already exists in the spec, just the later sections weren't updated.
title: Fix stale proto references in session-protocol spec (lines 611, 648, 654)

description:
Implement hit-test region tracking and spatial query per scene-graph/spec.md and input-model/spec.md. This is the pure data model for hit-testing — no input event processing.

## Spec References

### Requirement: Hit-Testing Contract (scene-graph/spec.md line 250)
Hit-testing maps a 2D point to the deepest interactive element. Traversal order: (1) Chrome layer first, (2) Content layer tiles by z_order descending, (3) within each tile, reverse tree order of nodes. Passthrough tiles skipped. HitRegionNodes are the interactive primitive. Hit-test returns one of: NodeHit, TileHit, Passthrough, or Chrome.
Source: RFC 0001 §5.1, §5.2 | Scope: v1-mandatory

### Requirement: Hit-Test Performance (scene-graph/spec.md line 267)
Hit-testing MUST complete in < 100us for a single point query against 50 tiles, measured as pure Rust execution with no GPU involvement.
Source: RFC 0001 §5.1, §10 | Scope: v1-mandatory

### Requirement: HitRegionNode Primitive (input-model/spec.md line 248)
HitRegionNode is the sole interactive primitive in v1. Carries: bounds (relative to tile origin), interaction_id (agent-defined, forwarded in events), accepts_focus, accepts_pointer, auto_capture, release_on_up, cursor_style, tooltip (shown after 500ms hover), event_mask, accessibility metadata, local_style configuration.
Source: RFC 0004 §7.1, RFC 0001 §2.4 | Scope: v1-mandatory

### Requirement: V1 Node Types (scene-graph/spec.md line 125, HitRegionNode subset)
HitRegionNode is a leaf node type with bounds, interaction_id, accepts_focus, accepts_pointer. Each node has a SceneId and ordered list of child SceneIds.
Source: RFC 0001 §2.4 | Scope: v1-mandatory

## WHEN/THEN Scenarios from Spec

- Scenario: Chrome layer always wins (line 255)
  WHEN a pointer event lands on a chrome element (tab bar, system indicator)
  THEN the hit-test MUST return ChromeHit regardless of underlying tiles

- Scenario: Highest-z tile hit (line 259)
  WHEN two non-passthrough tiles overlap and the pointer lands in the overlap region
  THEN the hit-test MUST return a hit for the tile with the higher z_order

- Scenario: Passthrough tile skipped (line 263)
  WHEN a pointer event lands on a tile with input_mode = Passthrough
  THEN the hit-test MUST skip that tile and test the next tile in z-order

- Scenario: Hit-test latency benchmark (line 272)
  WHEN a hit-test query is executed against a scene with 50 tiles
  THEN the query MUST complete in less than 100us on reference hardware (single core at 3GHz equivalent)

- Scenario: HitRegionNode local state (scene-graph/spec.md line 138)
  WHEN a pointer hovers over a HitRegionNode with accepts_pointer = true
  THEN the runtime MUST update the node's local hovered state for immediate visual feedback without waiting for agent acknowledgement

- Scenario: Event mask filters delivery (input-model/spec.md line 253)
  WHEN a HitRegionNode has event_mask.pointer_move=false
  THEN PointerMoveEvent SHALL NOT be dispatched to the owning agent for that node

- Scenario: interaction_id forwarded in events (input-model/spec.md line 257)
  WHEN a HitRegionNode has interaction_id="submit-button" and receives a ClickEvent
  THEN the ClickEvent SHALL include interaction_id="submit-button"

- Scenario: Chrome layer always wins hit-test (input-model/spec.md line 272)
  WHEN a click lands on a pixel where a chrome element and a content tile overlap
  THEN the chrome element SHALL win the hit-test

## Implementation Details

Hit-test is a pure spatial query: given (x, y) display coordinates, traverse Chrome → Content tiles (z-order desc) → Nodes (reverse tree order) and return the first HitRegionNode whose bounds contain the point. No GPU involvement — pure geometry. HitResult enum: NodeHit { tile_id, node_id, interaction_id }, TileHit { tile_id }, Passthrough, Chrome { element_id }. Local state (hovered, pressed) tracked on HitRegionNodes for immediate visual feedback without agent roundtrip (doctrine: local feedback first).

## Acceptance Criteria

- input_highlight test scene passes Layer 0 invariants
- Hit-test correctness for overlapping regions verified by property tests: proptest generates random tile layouts with HitRegionNodes, verifies chrome-first, z-order-descending, reverse-tree-order traversal
- Hit-test returns correct result type (NodeHit, TileHit, Passthrough, Chrome) for all cases
- Passthrough tiles are skipped in traversal
- Performance: < 100us for point query against 50 tiles (benchmarked, hardware-normalized)
- Local state (hovered/pressed) updated without agent roundtrip
- Event mask consulted but not processed (this bead defines the mask, input processing is separate epic)

## Epic 0 Test Gates

- assert_layer0_invariants() on input_highlight test scene
- Hit-test proptest suite: random scene layouts, verify traversal correctness
- Test scenes most relevant: input_highlight, overlay_passthrough_regions, overlapping_tiles_zorder, chatty_dashboard_touch, three_agents_contention

## Crate/File Location

- Primary: crates/tze_hud_scene/src/graph.rs (hit_test method on SceneGraph)
- Types: crates/tze_hud_scene/src/types.rs (HitResult enum, HitRegionNode fields)
- Benchmarks: crates/tze_hud_scene/benches/hit_test.rs (criterion benchmark for < 100us)
- Tests: crates/tze_hud_scene/tests/hit_test.rs (integration tests)

## v1 Scope Tags

- v1-mandatory: Hit-Testing Contract (scene-graph/spec.md line 250), Hit-Test Performance (line 267), HitRegionNode Primitive (input-model/spec.md line 248)
- v1-reserved: None for this bead
- post-v1: Spatial acceleration structures (quadtree/BVH for scenes with hundreds of tiles), accessibility hit-testing

## What This Does NOT Include

- Input event processing/dispatch (input model epic) — this bead is the spatial query only, not event routing
- HitRegionNode definition as a node type (bead #2) — consumed here, defined there
- Focus management state machine (input model epic) — this bead tracks local hovered/pressed state, not focus chains
- Pointer capture semantics (input model epic)
- Zone layer attachment routing in hit-test (bead #4 defines layers, this bead queries them)
- Rendering of focus indicators (compositor epic)

description: During review of PR #155 (pointer event dispatch pipeline), two threads identified that timestamp_mono_us fields in PointerFields and EventBatch.batch_ts_us use raw u64 instead of the MonoUs newtype from tze_hud_scene::timing::domains. The MonoUs newtype enforces clock-domain correctness at compile time. This migration is cross-cutting: all event types in pointer.rs, events.rs, and callers must be updated together.

description:
Implement subscription-based event delivery per scene-events/spec.md, including category filtering, self-event suppression, event coalescing under backpressure, the four-stage pipeline, and dual routing with deduplication.

## Spec Requirements

### Requirement: Subscription Model with Category Filtering (spec lines 156-184)
9 subscription categories with category-to-event-type-prefix mapping:
- SCENE_TOPOLOGY -> scene.* (TileCreated, TileDeleted, TabCreated, AgentJoined, ZoneOccupancyChanged)
- INPUT_EVENTS -> input.* (governed by RFC 0004)
- FOCUS_EVENTS -> scene.focus.*
- DEGRADATION_NOTICES -> system.degradation_* (mandatory, cannot opt out)
- LEASE_CHANGES -> system.lease_* (mandatory, cannot opt out)
- ZONE_EVENTS -> scene.zone.* (ZoneOccupancyChanged also routed here)
- TELEMETRY_FRAMES -> system.telemetry_*
- ATTENTION_EVENTS -> system.attention_*
- AGENT_EVENTS -> agent.*

Finer-grained filters use event type prefix matching with trailing wildcards (e.g., "scene.zone.*").
Maximum subscriptions per agent: 32.
DEGRADATION_NOTICES and LEASE_CHANGES always subscribed (cannot opt out).
Other categories filtered by agent's granted capabilities.

### Requirement: Self-Event Suppression (spec lines 254-266)
Runtime MUST NOT deliver a SceneEvent to the agent whose MutationBatch caused the state change. Lease and degradation events exempt (always delivered even if self-triggered). Self-event suppression check < 1 microsecond per event.

### Requirement: Event Bus Four-Stage Pipeline (spec lines 187-194)
Events flow through: (1) Emission, (2) Classification (<5us), (3) Policy Filtering, (4) Delivery fan-out (<100us per subscriber).

### Requirement: Event Classification Latency (spec lines 198-206)
Classification (Stage 2) < 5 microseconds per event.

### Requirement: Event Delivery Latency (spec lines 209-216)
Delivery to subscriber (Stage 4) < 100 microseconds from emission.

### Requirement: Event Coalescing Under Backpressure (spec lines 220-232)
Under backpressure, coalesce events with same coalesce key (e.g., tile_id for TileUpdated, singleton for ActiveTabChanged). Lease and degradation events never dropped. Coalesce buffer per subscriber bounded to 64 entries.

### Requirement: ZoneOccupancyChanged Dual Routing with Deduplication (spec lines 306-313)
ZoneOccupancyChanged appears in both SCENE_TOPOLOGY and ZONE_EVENTS. Agent subscribing to both receives it exactly once (runtime deduplicates before delivery).

### Requirement: Aggregate Event Rate Cap (spec lines 296-302)
Maximum aggregate event rate: 1000 events/second across all sources. Above this, backpressure or shedding applied.

### Requirement: Audit Event Visibility Restrictions (spec lines 280-293)
Shell-state audit events (safe mode, freeze, override) are system events with CRITICAL class. Always logged. NOT delivered to agents. Agents receive downstream effects (LeaseSuspendedEvent, LeaseResumedEvent, DegradationLevelChanged) but NOT raw override commands (system.safe_mode_entered, freeze, dismiss, mute).

## WHEN/THEN Scenarios (from spec)

- WHEN an agent has not subscribed to scene_topology THEN the runtime MUST NOT deliver TileCreated, TileDeleted, TabCreated, AgentJoined, or any other scene_topology events to that agent (spec line 16)
- WHEN an agent attempts to unsubscribe from LEASE_CHANGES or DEGRADATION_NOTICES THEN the runtime MUST reject the unsubscription; these categories cannot be opted out of (spec line 174)
- WHEN an agent subscribes to scene_topology with filter "scene.zone.*" THEN the agent MUST receive ZoneOccupancyChanged events but MUST NOT receive TileCreated or ActiveTabChanged events (spec line 179)
- WHEN an agent attempts to create a 33rd subscription (exceeding the limit of 32) THEN the excess subscriptions MUST be denied in SubscriptionChangeResult.denied_subscriptions (spec line 183)
- WHEN Agent A commits a MutationBatch that creates a tile THEN Agent A MUST NOT receive the TileCreated event; it has ground truth from BatchCommitted (spec line 261)
- WHEN Agent A's budget violation triggers revocation of Agent A's own lease THEN Agent A MUST still receive the LeaseRevokedEvent so it knows its lease is gone (spec line 265)
- WHEN an agent emits a scene event THEN the event MUST pass through emission, classification, policy filtering, and delivery stages in that order before reaching any subscriber (spec line 194)
- WHEN any event passes through the classification stage THEN the classification MUST complete within 5 microseconds (spec line 205)
- WHEN a TileCreated event is emitted during the compositor's mutation commit THEN the event MUST be enqueued on each matching subscriber's outbound stream within 100 microseconds of emission (spec line 216)
- WHEN a subscriber's outbound buffer is full and multiple TileUpdated events arrive for the same tile_id THEN only the latest TileUpdated for that tile_id MUST be retained; earlier updates MUST be coalesced away (spec line 227)
- WHEN a subscriber is under extreme backpressure and a LeaseRevokedEvent arrives THEN the LeaseRevokedEvent MUST NOT be dropped or coalesced away (spec line 231)
- WHEN an agent subscribes to both scene_topology and zone_events, and a ZoneOccupancyChanged event occurs THEN the agent MUST receive the event exactly once, not twice (spec line 313)
- WHEN total event generation across all sources exceeds 1000 events/second sustained THEN the runtime MUST apply backpressure or shedding to bring the rate within budget (spec line 302)
- WHEN the viewer enters safe mode THEN agents MUST receive LeaseSuspendedEvent for their leases but MUST NOT receive the system.safe_mode_entered event (spec line 287)
- WHEN the scene is frozen by the viewer THEN agents MUST NOT be informed of the freeze; their mutations SHALL be silently queued (spec line 291)

## Acceptance Criteria

- Category-to-capability mapping matches spec table (9 categories mapped)
- DEGRADATION_NOTICES and LEASE_CHANGES always delivered regardless of subscription state
- Unsubscribed categories not delivered
- Finer-grained prefix filters (e.g., "scene.zone.*") restrict delivery within a category
- 32 subscription limit enforced; excess denied
- Self-event suppression: agent does not receive events from its own MutationBatch
- Self-event exemption: lease and degradation events delivered even to causer
- Self-suppression check < 1us per event
- Four-stage pipeline ordering enforced
- Classification < 5us, delivery < 100us per subscriber
- Coalescing by coalesce key (tile_id, singleton) under backpressure
- Lease/degradation events never dropped under backpressure
- Coalesce buffer bounded at 64 entries per subscriber
- ZoneOccupancyChanged deduplicated across SCENE_TOPOLOGY and ZONE_EVENTS
- Aggregate rate cap at 1000 events/second
- Audit events (safe mode, freeze) not delivered to agents; downstream effects delivered

## Relevant test scenes
- chatty_dashboard_touch (backpressure, coalescing)
- three_agents_contention (multi-agent delivery, self-suppression)
- zone_conflict_two_publishers (zone events, dual routing)
- disconnect_reclaim_multiagent (lease events always delivered)
- policy_arbitration_collision (audit visibility, quiet hours interaction)

## Crate/file location
- Subscription registry: crates/tze_hud_runtime/src/subscriptions.rs
- Event bus pipeline: crates/tze_hud_runtime/src/event_bus.rs
- Coalescing: crates/tze_hud_runtime/src/event_bus/coalesce.rs
- Self-suppression: crates/tze_hud_runtime/src/event_bus/suppression.rs
- Deduplication: crates/tze_hud_runtime/src/event_bus/dedup.rs

## v1-mandatory
- Subscription Model with Category Filtering (spec lines 156-184)
- Self-Event Suppression (spec lines 254-266)
- Event Bus Four-Stage Pipeline (spec lines 187-194)
- Event Classification Latency (spec lines 198-206)
- Event Delivery Latency (spec lines 209-216)
- Event Coalescing Under Backpressure (spec lines 220-232)
- ZoneOccupancyChanged Dual Routing with Deduplication (spec lines 306-313)
- Aggregate Event Rate Cap (spec lines 296-302)
- Audit Event Visibility Restrictions (spec lines 280-293)

## v1-reserved exclusions
None for this bead.

## post-v1 exclusions
- Inter-Agent Event Bus (spec lines 328-335)
- Advanced Event Routing (spec lines 339-346)

## What this bead does NOT include
- Event envelope structure (bead #1)
- Naming convention validation (bead #1)
- Interruption classification and quiet hours enforcement (bead #3)
- Agent event emission protocol and rate limiting (bead #4)
- tab_switch_on_event (bead #4)
title: Subscription filtering and delivery

description:
Expand TestSceneRegistry from 5 to 25 named test scenes as pure data structures in crates/tze_hud_scene/src/test_scenes.rs. No GPU, no rendering — pure SceneSpec + SceneGraph data.

## Spec Links

- validation-framework/spec.md line 160: canonical list of 25 scene names
- heart-and-soul/validation.md DR-V4 (lines 73-86): deterministic test scenes, named, versioned, all randomness seeded, clocks injectable
- heart-and-soul/validation.md DR-V1 (lines 73-86): scene model separable from renderer (pure data structure)
- heart-and-soul/validation.md lines 170-201: scene registry defines expectations ACROSS ALL FIVE LAYERS (scene graph, synthetic content, Layer 0 invariants, Layer 1 pixel expectations, Layer 2 golden, Layer 3 budgets, Layer 4 explanation)
- scene-graph/spec.md lines 45-64: scene hierarchy (Scene → Tab[0-256] → Tile[0-1024] → Node[0-64])
- scene-graph/spec.md lines 125-140: node types (SolidColorNode, TextMarkdownNode, StaticImageNode, HitRegionNode)
- scene-graph/spec.md lines 142-157: atomic batch semantics for coalesced_dashboard scene
- scene-graph/spec.md lines 185-200: zone registry and zone publishing for zone_* scenes
- lease-governance/spec.md lines 10-25: lease state machine for lease_expiry scene (ACTIVE→EXPIRED on TTL elapse)
- lease-governance/spec.md lines 132-155: orphan grace period for disconnect_reclaim_multiagent scene (30,000ms, heartbeat-based detection)
- lease-governance/spec.md lines 62-69: priority sort for three_agents_contention scene (lease_priority ASC, z_order DESC)
- policy-arbitration/spec.md lines 10-17: seven-level stack for policy_matrix_basic scene
- policy-arbitration/spec.md lines 91-104: Level 2 Privacy Evaluation for privacy_redaction_mode scene (VisibilityClassification vs ViewerClass)
- policy-arbitration/spec.md lines 194-199: per-frame evaluation order for policy_arbitration_collision scene
- scene-events/spec.md lines 50-65: interruption classes for input_highlight scene
- input-model/spec.md lines 11-22: focus tree for input_highlight scene (per-tab, ≤1 focus owner)
- input-model/spec.md lines 78-89: focus cycling for input_highlight scene (z-ascending, depth-first)
- input-model/spec.md lines 104-125: pointer capture for overlay_passthrough_regions scene
- input-model/spec.md line 264: hit-test pipeline for overlay_passthrough_regions (chrome-first, z-descending)
- resource-store/spec.md lines 31-42: v1 resource types for static image nodes
- configuration/spec.md lines 123-134: zone registry (subtitle, notification, status_bar, pip, ambient_background, alert_banner)
- configuration/spec.md lines 49-82: built-in profiles for mobile_degraded scene (full-display, headless, mobile rejection)
- timing-model/spec.md lines 124-173: sync groups for sync_group_media scene (create, join, commit policies AllOrDefer/AvailableMembers)
- timing-model/spec.md lines 50-61: present_at semantics for sync_group_media scene (future → pending queue)

## Scenes to Add (20 new, 5 existing)

Existing (retain): empty_scene, single_tile_solid, two_tiles (→ three_tiles_no_overlap), max_tiles_stress, zone_test (→ zone_publish_subtitle)

New scenes with spec cross-references:
1. overlapping_tiles_zorder — 3 tiles with overlapping bounds, explicit z-order; validates z-order composition per scene-graph/spec.md lines 45-64
2. overlay_transparency — chrome overlay with alpha < 1.0 over agent tile; validates alpha blending path per heart-and-soul/validation.md line 117 (±2/channel tolerance)
3. tab_switch — 2 tabs with different tile layouts; validates tab isolation per scene-graph/spec.md lines 45-64 (Tab[0-256])
4. lease_expiry — tile with TTL set via grant_lease_at(); validates ACTIVE→EXPIRED transition per lease-governance/spec.md lines 10-25
5. mobile_degraded — profile=mobile constraints; validates degradation path per configuration/spec.md lines 71-82 (mobile profile rejection in v1)
6. sync_group_media — 2 tiles in sync group with staggered present_at; validates sync group commit per timing-model/spec.md lines 124-173 (AllOrDefer policy, max_defer_frames=3)
7. input_highlight — tile with HitRegionNode accepting focus + pointer; validates focus tree per input-model/spec.md lines 11-22 and focus cycling per lines 78-89
8. coalesced_dashboard — 10+ tiles, rapid mutation stream; validates state-stream coalescing per scene-graph/spec.md lines 142-157 (atomic batch)
9. three_agents_contention — 3 agents with overlapping z-order requests; validates priority sort per lease-governance/spec.md lines 62-69 (lease_priority ASC, z_order DESC)
10. overlay_passthrough_regions — chrome overlay with passthrough regions; validates hit-test pipeline per input-model/spec.md line 264 (chrome-first, z-descending, reverse tree order)
11. disconnect_reclaim_multiagent — 3 agents, 1 disconnects; validates orphan detection + grace period per lease-governance/spec.md lines 132-155 (30,000ms grace, heartbeat timeout 3×5000ms)
12. privacy_redaction_mode — tiles with VisibilityClassification vs ViewerClass; validates redaction per policy-arbitration/spec.md lines 91-104 (Level 2 Privacy Evaluation)
13. chatty_dashboard_touch — high-frequency touch events on dashboard layout; validates input drain budget per input-model/spec.md line 264 (<100µs hit-test for 50 tiles)
14. zone_publish_subtitle — (rename existing zone_test) tile publishing to subtitle zone per scene-graph/spec.md lines 198-200
15. zone_reject_wrong_type — attempt to publish wrong content type to typed zone per scene-graph/spec.md lines 198-200 (type validation)
16. zone_conflict_two_publishers — 2 agents publishing to same zone with LatestWins contention per scene-graph/spec.md lines 185-196
17. zone_orchestrate_then_publish — orchestrated zone publish sequence per scene-graph/spec.md lines 185-200
18. zone_geometry_adapts_profile — zone with geometry policy adapting to profile per configuration/spec.md lines 123-134
19. zone_disconnect_cleanup — zone publisher disconnects; validates cleanup per lease-governance/spec.md lines 132-155
20. policy_matrix_basic — scene triggering multiple policy levels; validates evaluation order per policy-arbitration/spec.md lines 194-199 (Level 1→2→5→6 per-frame)

## WHEN/THEN Scenarios Encoded

- WHEN empty scene is built THEN tab_count=0, tile_count=0, no hit regions, no zones
- WHEN single_tile_solid is built THEN exactly 1 tab, 1 tile, tile has SolidColorNode root
- WHEN overlapping_tiles_zorder is built THEN 3 tiles with distinct z-orders, bounds overlap verified
- WHEN lease_expiry scene is built with ClockMs::FIXED THEN tile has ACTIVE lease; after clock advance past TTL, lease state = EXPIRED
- WHEN zone_reject_wrong_type is built THEN zone publish with mismatched type is rejected
- WHEN privacy_redaction_mode is built THEN tiles with SENSITIVE classification are present for redaction testing
- WHEN sync_group_media is built THEN 2 tiles share sync_group_id, present_at timestamps differ, commit policy = AllOrDefer
- WHEN three_agents_contention is built THEN 3 distinct namespaces, overlapping tile bounds, different lease priorities
- WHEN disconnect_reclaim_multiagent is built THEN 3 agents, 1 agent's lease can be orphaned via clock advance past heartbeat timeout
- WHEN coalesced_dashboard is built THEN ≥10 tiles with rapid sequential mutations demonstrate coalescing path
- WHEN input_highlight is built THEN tile has HitRegionNode with accepts_focus=true, auto_capture=true
- WHEN overlay_passthrough_regions is built THEN chrome overlay has mixed passthrough/interactive regions for hit-test validation
- WHEN chatty_dashboard_touch is built THEN dashboard layout with HitRegionNode tiles ready for high-frequency input injection
- WHEN policy_matrix_basic is built THEN scene includes viewer class, content classification, interruption class, and degradation state sufficient to exercise all 7 policy levels

## SceneSpec Design Note

Per validation.md lines 170-201, each scene definition is the ground truth across all five validation layers. SceneSpec may need expansion to include:
- expected_pixel_regions: Vec<(Rect, ExpectedColor)> — for Layer 1 pixel readback
- expected_budgets: BudgetExpectations — for Layer 3 telemetry
- layer0_invariant_ids: Vec<InvariantId> — which invariants must hold
These can be added as companion data or SceneSpec field expansions, ensuring each scene is self-documenting across layers.

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] scene_names() returns exactly 25 entries
- [ ] Each scene has a complete SceneSpec (name, description, expected_tab_count, expected_tile_count, has_hit_regions, has_zones)
- [ ] Each scene builds successfully via TestSceneRegistry::new().build(name, ClockMs::FIXED)
- [ ] Each scene specifies Layer 0 invariants that must hold (documented in SceneSpec or companion comments)
- [ ] All scenes use ClockMs for deterministic time injection (DR-V4)
- [ ] #[test] function per scene verifying it builds without error
- [ ] No GPU, no rendering, no HeadlessRuntime dependency — pure data validation only

## Crate/File Location

- Primary: crates/tze_hud_scene/src/test_scenes.rs (expand existing file)
- Tests: inline #[cfg(test)] mod in same file

## What This Does NOT Include

This bead produces TEST SCENE DATA DEFINITIONS only. It does NOT:
- Implement compositor rendering for new scenes
- Add GPU/headless rendering tests (that is bead #5)
- Implement lease state machine logic (that is implementation work)
- Add property-based tests (that is bead #3)
- Modify HeadlessRuntime or telemetry infrastructure
