description: The session-protocol spec (Input Control Request Transport requirement) claims InputFocusRequest=26, InputCaptureRequest=27, InputCaptureRelease=28, SetImePosition=29 for ClientMessage, but session.proto has telemetry_frame=26 at that field. Additionally, the spec claims InputFocusResponse=43, InputCaptureResponse=44 for ServerMessage, but ServerMessage only defines fields 10-35. Fix: add input control fields to ClientMessage at 27-29 (after telemetry_frame=26), update spec to match (InputFocusRequest=27, etc.), and add InputFocusResponse/InputCaptureResponse to ServerMessage at fields in the 36-49 reserved range. Discovered during review of PR #98 (rig-4fph).
title: Reconcile input control field numbers between spec and session.proto

description:
Direct v1 contradiction:

**scene-graph/spec.md line 325:**
'Durable resources (images, fonts) SHALL survive restart because they are content-addressed and independent of scene graph state.'

**resource-store/spec.md lines 244-245:**
'In v1, all resources SHALL be ephemeral. They SHALL be stored in memory and lost on runtime restart.'

**resource-store/spec.md lines 373-375:**
'A durable persistent resource store surviving runtime restarts is deferred to post-v1.'

**Resolution:** resource-store is the authoritative owner of resource lifecycle semantics. Resources are ephemeral in v1. Update scene-graph/spec.md line 325 to remove the 'survive restart' claim and instead state that resources are content-addressed and deduplicated but ephemeral in v1 (persistence deferred to post-v1 per resource-store spec). Scene snapshots should be agnostic to resource persistence — they reference ResourceIds but don't guarantee the backing data survives restart.
title: Fix resource persistence contradiction between scene-graph and resource-store specs

description:
Define expected pixel assertions for all 25 test scenes with visual output. Pattern: render scene → read pixels via HeadlessSurface::read_pixels() → assert region colors within tolerance. Extends the 3 existing pixel tests in examples/vertical_slice/tests/budget_assertions.rs.

## Spec Links

- validation-framework/spec.md Layer 1 (heart-and-soul/validation.md lines 109-122): headless render + pixel readback
- heart-and-soul/validation.md DR-V2 (lines 73-86): headless rendering, offscreen, no GPU/display server required
- heart-and-soul/validation.md DR-V6 (lines 73-86): no physical GPU for CI (mesa llvmpipe or SwiftShader)
- heart-and-soul/validation.md line 117: alpha tolerance ±2/channel software GPU, ±1 solid fills
- scene-graph/spec.md lines 125-140: node types define visual output (SolidColorNode color, TextMarkdownNode, StaticImageNode)
- scene-graph/spec.md lines 45-64: z-order determines rendering order
- scene-graph/spec.md lines 185-200: zone rendering (subtitle text, notification stack, status bar)
- policy-arbitration/spec.md lines 91-104: Level 2 Privacy Evaluation — redaction via VisibilityClassification vs ViewerClass (neutral pattern, no agent content visible)
- input-model/spec.md lines 11-22: focus indicators (visual highlight on focused tile)
- input-model/spec.md line 264: hit-test performance <100µs for 50 tiles
- configuration/spec.md lines 123-134: zone types and their visual representations

## Frame Pipeline Context (RFC 0002)

Pixel readback MUST occur after all render stages complete. The runtime frame pipeline (RFC 0002 §2) defines 5 stages before present:
1. Input drain + local feedback (<1ms) — local feedback updates visual state WITHOUT agent roundtrip
2. Scene commit (<1ms)
3. Layout resolve (<2ms)
4. Render encode (<4ms)
5. GPU submit (<8ms)

Tests must ensure GPU work is fully submitted before readback. Use device.poll(wgpu::Maintain::Wait) or equivalent synchronization to avoid reading stale pixel state. The input_highlight scene's local feedback (focus ring, press state) has a p99 < 4ms budget from hardware event to visual update (RFC 0004 §6, heart-and-soul/validation.md line 133).

## Existing Infrastructure

- HeadlessRuntime: crates/tze_hud_runtime/src/headless.rs (lines 14-107)
- HeadlessSurface.read_pixels() → Vec<u8> RGBA8
- HeadlessSurface.assert_pixel_color(pixels, width, x, y, expected_rgba, tolerance, label) helper
- HeadlessConfig: width, height, grpc_port, psk
- 3 existing pixel tests in budget_assertions.rs:
  - test_background_color (lines 364-396): ±6/channel for llvmpipe
  - test_tile_background_color (lines 403-472): ±8/channel
  - test_zorder_overlap (lines 478-553): high-z occludes low-z

## Pixel Tolerance Standards

Per validation-framework/spec.md and existing tests:
- Solid fills: ±1/channel (ideal), ±6/channel (llvmpipe CI fallback)
- Alpha blending: ±2/channel (software GPU)
- Text presence: check for non-background pixels in expected text region (not exact glyph matching)
- Use CI_TOLERANCE constant pattern from existing tests for environment-adaptive thresholds
- Tolerance hierarchy: spec defines ideal (±1/±2); CI reality requires wider tolerance for llvmpipe (±6/±8 per existing tests). Define both IDEAL_TOLERANCE and CI_TOLERANCE constants; tests assert against CI_TOLERANCE but document IDEAL_TOLERANCE as the target.

## Test Definitions by Scene Category

### Solid Color & Basic Rendering (5 scenes)
1. **empty_scene**: background color matches runtime default at all sampled positions
2. **single_tile_solid**: tile region has specified color; outside tile is background
3. **three_tiles_no_overlap**: each tile region has its color; gaps between tiles are background
4. **overlapping_tiles_zorder**: overlap region has HIGH-z tile's color; non-overlap regions have each tile's color
5. **max_tiles_stress**: spot-check N tiles at known grid positions; background visible in gaps

### Alpha & Overlay (2 scenes)
6. **overlay_transparency**: chrome overlay region shows blended color (alpha composite of overlay + tile behind); pure tile region unaffected; tolerance ±2/channel
7. **overlay_passthrough_regions**: passthrough regions show underlying tile (no overlay tint); non-passthrough show overlay blend

### Tab & Layout (2 scenes)
8. **tab_switch**: after switch, active tab tiles visible, inactive tab tiles NOT rendered (background in their region)
9. **coalesced_dashboard**: verify N tiles rendered at expected positions after mutation storm settles

### Zone Rendering (6 scenes)
10. **zone_publish_subtitle**: subtitle zone region contains non-background pixels (text present)
11. **zone_reject_wrong_type**: no visual change (wrong-type publish rejected, previous state maintained)
12. **zone_conflict_two_publishers**: zone shows latest publisher's content (LatestWins)
13. **zone_orchestrate_then_publish**: zone shows orchestrated content after sequence completes
14. **zone_geometry_adapts_profile**: zone rendered at profile-appropriate position/size
15. **zone_disconnect_cleanup**: zone region returns to empty/default after publisher disconnect

### Privacy & Redaction (1 scene)
16. **privacy_redaction_mode**: ALL agent content regions show neutral/redaction pattern (solid gray or pattern fill); no agent-specified colors visible; chrome layer still renders normally on top. Per policy-arbitration/spec.md lines 91-104 (Level 2 Privacy Evaluation).

### Multi-Agent & Lease (3 scenes)
17. **lease_expiry**: before expiry, tile visible; after clock advance past TTL, tile removed (background visible)
18. **three_agents_contention**: each agent's tiles visible in their respective regions; arbitration-denied tiles not rendered
19. **disconnect_reclaim_multiagent**: disconnected agent's tiles removed after grace period; other agents' tiles unaffected

### Chrome & Input (2 scenes)
20. **input_highlight**: focused tile has visual focus indicator (distinct border/highlight color in tile edge region). Local feedback must render without agent roundtrip (RFC 0004 §6).
21. **chatty_dashboard_touch**: tiles still render correctly after high-frequency input processing (no corruption)

### Degradation & Sync (2 scenes)
22. **mobile_degraded**: reduced tile count/complexity matches mobile profile constraints; tiles render at correct degraded positions
23. **sync_group_media**: both sync group tiles rendered after sync commit; verify both present simultaneously

### Policy (1 scene)
24. **policy_matrix_basic**: tiles visible/hidden based on policy evaluation result (system chrome always on top)

### Cross-cutting
25. **policy_arbitration_collision**: guest agent + urgent notification + degradation + quiet hours — validates canonical policy evaluation order end-to-end (policy-arbitration/spec.md lines 10-17)

## WHEN/THEN Scenarios

- WHEN empty_scene rendered THEN all sampled pixels match background RGBA within ±1/channel
- WHEN single_tile_solid rendered THEN tile region center pixel matches tile color within ±1/channel
- WHEN overlapping_tiles_zorder rendered THEN overlap region matches high-z tile color, non-overlap matches respective tile colors
- WHEN overlay_transparency rendered THEN overlay region pixel = alpha_blend(overlay_color, tile_color) within ±2/channel
- WHEN tab_switch to tab B rendered THEN tab A tile positions show background, tab B tile positions show tile colors
- WHEN privacy_redaction_mode rendered THEN agent tile regions show redaction color (not agent's specified color), chrome renders normally
- WHEN lease_expiry scene rendered after TTL THEN expired tile region shows background
- WHEN zone_publish_subtitle rendered THEN subtitle region has non-background pixels in text area
- WHEN disconnect_reclaim_multiagent rendered after grace THEN disconnected agent's region shows background
- WHEN input_highlight rendered THEN focused tile edge pixels differ from unfocused tile edge pixels (focus indicator visible)

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Pixel assertion test function defined for each of the 25 test scenes (some scenes share assertion patterns)
- [ ] ≥25 #[test] functions total (one per scene minimum)
- [ ] Each test uses HeadlessRuntime with deterministic config (ClockMs::FIXED, fixed dimensions)
- [ ] Tolerance constants defined: IDEAL_SOLID_TOLERANCE (±1), IDEAL_BLEND_TOLERANCE (±2), CI_SOLID_TOLERANCE (±6), CI_BLEND_TOLERANCE (±8) with llvmpipe fallback
- [ ] Tests use assert_pixel_color() helper for consistent assertion format
- [ ] Each test includes descriptive label strings for failure diagnostics
- [ ] Tests ensure GPU synchronization before readback (device.poll(Maintain::Wait) or equivalent)
- [ ] Tests are #[ignore]d or feature-gated (#[cfg(feature = "headless")]) until compositor features land
- [ ] No compositor implementation — tests define EXPECTED VALUES only

## Crate/File Location

- Primary: crates/tze_hud_runtime/tests/pixel_readback.rs (new integration test file)
- Shared helpers: crates/tze_hud_runtime/tests/pixel_helpers.rs (tolerance constants, region sampling utilities)
- Uses scenes from test_scenes.rs (bead #1 dependency)
- Uses HeadlessRuntime from crates/tze_hud_runtime/src/headless.rs

## What This Does NOT Include

This bead produces PIXEL TEST DEFINITIONS AND EXPECTED VALUES only. It does NOT:
- Implement compositor rendering features (tile rendering, zone rendering, alpha blending, redaction)
- Add Layer 0 logic tests (that is bead #3)
- Add protocol tests (that is bead #4)
- Modify HeadlessRuntime internals (only uses existing read_pixels API)
- Add Layer 2 visual regression (SSIM golden references) — that comes later
- Add Layer 3 performance/telemetry tests — existing budget_assertions.rs already covers those

description:
The runtime-kernel/spec.md Requirement: Window Modes (line 172-173) mandates two window modes. This bead implements the mode-specific behavior on top of the WindowedRuntime from its dependency.

**Fullscreen mode (all platforms):**
- Compositor owns entire display with opaque background
- All input captured (no passthrough)
- Scenario (line 177-179): WHEN fullscreen mode THEN compositor owns entire display, all input captured

**Overlay/HUD mode (platform-specific):**
- Transparent, borderless, always-on-top window
- Per-region input passthrough (events outside hit-regions pass to desktop)
- Platform implementations:
  - Windows: WM_NCHITTEST for per-pixel passthrough
  - macOS: NSWindow.ignoresMouseEvents + hitTest override
  - X11: XShape extension for passthrough regions
  - wlroots: wlr-layer-shell protocol
- Scenario (line 181-183): WHEN overlay mode AND pointer outside hit-regions THEN event passes through
- Scenario (line 185-187): WHEN overlay mode on GNOME Wayland (no layer-shell) THEN fall back to fullscreen with warning

**Runtime mode switching:**
- Supported but disruptive — requires surface recreation
- Not expected to be frequent in v1

**Spec refs:**
- runtime-kernel/spec.md Requirement: Window Modes (lines 172-188)
- tasks.md items 3.4 (fullscreen) and 3.5 (overlay/HUD)
- input-model/spec.md Requirement: Event Routing Resolution (line 316: overlay passthrough)

**Acceptance criteria:**
- Fullscreen mode renders full-viewport on all platforms (Linux, macOS, Windows)
- Overlay mode with click-through verified on at least one platform
- GNOME Wayland fallback to fullscreen with logged warning
- Mode selection via config or CLI flag

**v1-mandatory. Crate: crates/tze_hud_runtime/, platform-specific in window.rs**
title: Implement fullscreen and overlay/HUD window modes with platform-specific behavior

close_reason: Resolved by PR #52 (rig-5vq.13 R3) — redaction_style removed from [chrome], ownership assigned to [privacy] per RFC 0009 §3.2
description:
## Problem

`redaction_style` appears in two config sections:

1. `[privacy].redaction_style` (§7.1, line ~891): "Redaction style for tiles whose classification exceeds the viewer's access. One of: \"pattern\", \"agent_name\", \"icon\", \"blank\". Default: \"pattern\""

2. `[chrome].redaction_style` (§2.8, line ~281): "Redaction placeholder style. One of: \"pattern\", \"agent_name\", \"icon\". Default: \"pattern\""

The same concept — how redacted content is visually replaced — is configurable in two places with slightly different enum variants (`[privacy]` includes `"blank"`, `[chrome]` does not). This creates ambiguity: which value wins? If they disagree, what is the behavior? The RFC does not specify precedence.

## Affected Sections

- RFC 0006 §2.8 (`[chrome]` section, `redaction_style` field)
- RFC 0006 §7.1 (`[privacy]` section, `redaction_style` field)

## Doctrine Reference

- privacy.md §"Redaction behavior": "The specific redaction style is configurable" — singular, implying one config point
- architecture.md §"Configuration model": configuration is validated at load time — dual ownership makes validation ambiguous

## Required Change

1. Choose a single config owner for `redaction_style`. The natural home is `[privacy]` because redaction is a privacy concern (privacy.md §"Redaction behavior" defines the concept). The chrome layer renders it, but the policy belongs to privacy governance.
2. Remove `redaction_style` from `[chrome]`. The chrome layer reads the value from `[privacy].redaction_style` at render time.
3. Unify the enum variants: add `"blank"` to the canonical enum if it is a valid redaction mode, or remove it from `[privacy]` if it is not.
4. Add a validation rule: if both sections somehow contain `redaction_style` (e.g., from a config merge), emit `CONFIG_DUPLICATE_REDACTION_STYLE` and reject.
5. Update the `ChromeConfig` Rust struct to remove the `redaction_style` field. Add a cross-reference comment in `ChromeConfig` pointing to `PrivacyConfig.redaction_style`.
title: RFC 0006: fix dual ownership of redaction_style config

close_reason: PR #136 merged after PR review. Safe mode suspend/resume implementation reviewed, 12 issues found and fixed, all tests pass (24 safe_mode + 88 protocol).
description:
# Safe Mode: Suspend and Resume

Implement the safe mode protocol that suspends all agent leases (not revoke — identity preserved), renders a safe mode overlay, and restores leases on explicit viewer exit.

## Ownership rule
The shell is the SOLE owner of safe mode state transitions. Policy-arbitration evaluates triggers (Level 0 Human Override, Level 1 Safety) but does NOT transition shell state. Only the shell state machine writes OverrideState.safe_mode. The shell is also the sole writer of OverrideState.freeze_active — safe mode entry cancels any active freeze.

## Spec references (system-shell/spec.md)

### Requirement: Safe Mode Entry Protocol (line 89)
Safe mode enterable by: (1) Ctrl+Shift+Escape or "Dismiss All" chrome control (Level 0 Human Override), (2) automatic on critical runtime error (GPU device loss, scene graph corruption, unrecoverable render failure — Level 1 Safety). On entry: all active leases suspended (NOT revoked), all agent sessions receive SessionSuspended with reason safe_mode, safe mode overlay rendered, all input captured by chrome layer.
- Scenario: Manual safe mode entry (line 94) — WHEN viewer presses Ctrl+Shift+Escape THEN all active leases suspended, agents receive SessionSuspended, overlay appears, all input routes to chrome
- Scenario: Auto safe mode on GPU loss (line 98) — WHEN compositor detects wgpu::DeviceError::Lost THEN runtime enters safe mode with SafeModeEntryReason = CRITICAL_ERROR

### Requirement: Safe Mode Overlay (line 102)
Overlay displays: centered banner ("Safe Mode -- All agent sessions paused."), prominent "Resume" button, current viewer class indicator, no agent branding/content. Renders correctly even if scene graph corrupted (reads from ChromeState and theme colors/font atlas only).
- Scenario: Safe mode overlay rendering (line 107) — WHEN safe mode entered THEN full-viewport overlay with banner, Resume button, viewer class icon, no agent content
- Scenario: Overlay with corrupted scene graph (line 111) — WHEN safe mode entered due to scene graph corruption THEN overlay still renders correctly

### Requirement: Safe Mode Exit (line 115)
Exit only by explicit viewer action: click/tap "Resume", Enter/Space (resume button has focus by default), or Ctrl+Shift+Escape toggle. On exit: overlay dismissed, SUSPENDED leases transition to ACTIVE, sessions receive SessionResumed (empty message), each lease receives LeaseResume with adjusted_expires_at_wall_us and suspension_duration_us, TTL clocks resume with suspension time excluded, staleness badges clear within 1 frame, mutations accepted again, compositor resumes from current scene state. Agents do NOT re-request leases — identity, capability scope, and resource budget preserved across ACTIVE -> SUSPENDED -> ACTIVE cycle.
- Scenario: Resume from safe mode (line 120) — WHEN viewer clicks Resume THEN overlay dismissed, SUSPENDED leases to ACTIVE, SessionResumed sent, LeaseResume with adjusted expiry sent, staleness badges clear within 1 frame, mutations accepted without re-request

### Requirement: Safe Mode and Freeze Interaction (Shell State Invariant) (line 124)
State invariant: safe_mode=true implies freeze_active=false. The shell is the sole writer of OverrideState.freeze_active. On any safe mode activation: freeze cancelled, freeze queue discarded, freeze_active=false BEFORE any other safe mode entry steps. Freeze attempted during safe mode is ignored. After safe mode exit, freeze is inactive.
- Scenario: Safe mode cancels freeze (automatic trigger) (line 129) — WHEN scene frozen and GPU failure triggers safe mode THEN freeze cancelled, queue discarded, freeze_active=false, overlay appears
- Scenario: Safe mode cancels freeze (manual trigger) (line 133) — WHEN scene frozen and viewer triggers safe mode via Ctrl+Shift+Escape THEN freeze cancelled, queue discarded, freeze_active=false, overlay appears
- Scenario: Freeze ignored during safe mode (line 137) — WHEN viewer presses Ctrl+Shift+F while safe mode active THEN no effect; safe mode captures all input

## Secondary spec references

### lease-governance/spec.md
- Requirement: Safe Mode Suspends Leases (line 92) — all active leases transition to SUSPENDED on safe mode entry
- Requirement: Safe Mode Resume (line 105) — on exit, SUSPENDED leases transition to ACTIVE; SessionResumed and LeaseResume sent; resume within 2 frames (33.2ms)
- Requirement: Max Suspension Time (line 114) — maximum suspension duration before auto-resume
- Requirement: Suspension Preserves State (line 123) — lease identity/capabilities/budgets preserved

### policy-arbitration/spec.md
- Requirement: Level 0 Human Override (line 48) — policy evaluates Ctrl+Shift+Escape as Level 0 but the shell transitions state

## Acceptance criteria
- Safe mode suspends all active leases (SUSPENDED not REVOKED)
- SessionSuspended sent to all agent sessions with reason safe_mode
- Mutations rejected during safe mode
- Safe mode overlay renders correctly (banner, Resume button, viewer class, no agent content)
- Overlay renders correctly even with corrupted scene graph
- Exit via Resume/Enter/Space/Ctrl+Shift+Escape toggle restores leases to ACTIVE
- SessionResumed (empty) and LeaseResume (with adjusted TTL) sent on exit
- TTL pause verified: suspension time excluded from TTL accounting
- Staleness badges clear within 1 frame of resume
- Freeze cancelled on safe mode entry (freeze_active=false before other steps)
- Freeze queue discarded on safe mode entry
- Freeze attempt during safe mode ignored
- After safe mode exit, freeze is inactive
- Resume completes within 2 frames (33.2ms)
- Test scenes: policy_matrix_basic (safe mode overrides all policy levels)

## v1-mandatory requirements
- Safe Mode Entry Protocol (line 89)
- Safe Mode Overlay (line 102)
- Safe Mode Exit (line 115)
- Safe Mode and Freeze Interaction (Shell State Invariant) (line 124)

## v1-reserved exclusions
None for this bead.

## post-v1 exclusions
None for this bead.

## Crate/file location
crates/tze_hud_runtime/src/shell/safe_mode.rs — safe mode state machine, entry/exit logic, overlay rendering, freeze cancellation on entry

## What this does NOT include
- Chrome layer rendering (bead #1)
- Freeze queue implementation (bead #3)
- Privacy redaction (bead #4)
- Disconnection badges (bead #5)
- Lease state machine implementation (Epic 4)
- Policy evaluation logic (Epic 8)
title: Shell #2: Safe mode — suspend and resume

description:
## Problem

RFC 0007 (System Shell) defines two override states that affect the timing model but are not addressed by RFC 0003:

1. **Freeze (RFC 0007 Section 4.3):** Agent mutations are queued but not applied. The display shows the frozen scene. On unfreeze, queued mutations are applied in submission order.

2. **Safe mode (RFC 0007 Section 5):** All agent sessions receive SessionSuspended. Mutations are rejected with SAFE_MODE_ACTIVE until safe mode exits. On exit, pending mutations from the queue are applied.

RFC 0003 does not specify what happens to timing-sensitive state during these overrides:

- **present_at_us during freeze:** If a mutation has present_at_us = T and the scene is frozen at time T-1, does the mutation expire unused? Is T re-evaluated on unfreeze? The pending queue logic in Section 5.3 assumes normal frame progression.

- **expires_at_us during freeze:** If a tile has expires_at_us = T and the scene freezes at T-1, the expiry heap check (Section 5.4) would remove the tile on the next frame after T — but the tile's visual removal was deferred by freeze. Does the tile disappear instantly on unfreeze, or does freeze pause expiry evaluation?

- **Sync group deferral counters during freeze:** If an AllOrDefer group is mid-deferral when freeze activates, do the deferred_frames_count continue incrementing? If so, the group could force-commit while invisible, producing a state the viewer never saw.

- **Staleness indicators during freeze:** The tile_stale_threshold_ms timer (Section 4.7) would fire during freeze since mutations are queued, not applied. Should staleness indicators be suppressed during freeze?

- **Safe mode and session clocks:** During safe mode, sessions are suspended. Should the clock-skew estimation window (Section 4.3) be paused? Timestamps from a long safe mode period would skew the sliding window.

## Affected Sections

- Section 4.7 (Staleness Indicators) — needs freeze/safe-mode exception
- Section 5.3 (Presentation Deadline) — needs freeze behavior spec
- Section 5.4 (Expiration Policy) — needs freeze behavior spec
- Section 2.4 (Timing Contract) — needs freeze behavior for sync group deferral
- Section 4.3 (Drift Detection) — needs safe mode behavior for estimation window
- New subsection needed: 'Override State Interaction' or similar

## Doctrine Reference

architecture.md Section Policy arbitration: Human override always wins (position 1). RFC 0007 Section 4.5: Override effects appear within one frame. The timing model must not produce incorrect or surprising behavior when overrides are active.

## Concrete Change

Add a new section (e.g., Section 5.6 or a new top-level Section after Section 5) specifying:

1. **Freeze pauses timing evaluation.** present_at and expires_at evaluation is suspended during freeze. Expiry heap is not drained. Sync group deferral counters are paused. Staleness timers are paused. On unfreeze, timing resumes from the current wall clock — mutations whose present_at has passed are applied immediately; tiles whose expires_at has passed are expired immediately.

2. **Safe mode pauses per-session timing.** Clock-skew estimation window is frozen during SessionSuspended. On SessionResumed, the window is reset (not resumed with stale samples). Tiles with expires_at continue to be evaluated (safe mode does not prevent tile cleanup).

3. **Headless mode virtual clock.** Briefly note that headless mode with SimulatedClock does not have freeze/safe-mode concerns since the clock is manually advanced. But tests should cover freeze-then-advance-clock scenarios.

## Rationale

Freeze and safe mode are defined in the RFC set. The timing model must account for them or risk undefined behavior during override states.
title: RFC 0003: Define timer behavior during freeze and safe mode

description:
